&#x20;           const id = nextId++;

&#x20;           let resolve, reject;

&#x20;           const promise = new Promise((res, rej)=>{

&#x20;               resolve = res;

&#x20;               reject = rej;

&#x20;           });

&#x20;           requests.set(id, {

&#x20;               resolve,

&#x20;               reject

&#x20;           });

&#x20;           return ipc.send({

&#x20;               type: 'request',

&#x20;               id,

&#x20;               data: message

&#x20;           }).then(()=>promise);

&#x20;       },

&#x20;       sendError: (error)=>{

&#x20;           return ipc.sendError(error);

&#x20;       }

&#x20;   };

&#x20;   // Initialize module and send ready message

&#x20;   let getValue;

&#x20;   try {

&#x20;       const module = await moduleFactory();

&#x20;       if (typeof module.init === 'function') {

&#x20;           await module.init();

&#x20;       }

&#x20;       getValue = module.default;

&#x20;       await ipc.sendReady();

&#x20;   } catch (err) {

&#x20;       await ipc.sendReady();

&#x20;       await ipc.sendError(err);

&#x20;   }

&#x20;   // Queue handling

&#x20;   let isRunning = false;

&#x20;   const run = async ()=>{

&#x20;       while(queue.length > 0){

&#x20;           const args = queue.shift();

&#x20;           try {

&#x20;               const value = await getValue(internalIpc, ...args);

&#x20;               await ipc.send({

&#x20;                   type: 'end',

&#x20;                   data: value === undefined ? undefined : JSON.stringify(value, null, 2),

&#x20;                   duration: 0

&#x20;               });

&#x20;           } catch (e) {

&#x20;               await ipc.sendError(e);

&#x20;           }

&#x20;       }

&#x20;       isRunning = false;

&#x20;   };

&#x20;   // Communication handling

&#x20;   while(true){

&#x20;       const msg = await ipc.recv();

&#x20;       switch(msg.type){

&#x20;           case 'evaluate':

&#x20;               {

&#x20;                   queue.push(msg.args);

&#x20;                   if (!isRunning) {

&#x20;                       isRunning = true;

&#x20;                       run();

&#x20;                   }

&#x20;                   break;

&#x20;               }

&#x20;           case 'result':

&#x20;               {

&#x20;                   const request = requests.get(msg.id);

&#x20;                   if (request) {

&#x20;                       requests.delete(msg.id);

&#x20;                       if (msg.error) {

&#x20;                           request.reject(new Error(msg.error));

&#x20;                       } else {

&#x20;                           request.resolve(msg.data);

&#x20;                       }

&#x20;                   }

&#x20;                   break;

&#x20;               }

&#x20;           default:

&#x20;               {

&#x20;                   console.error('unexpected message type', msg.type);

&#x20;                   process.exit(1);

&#x20;               }

&#x20;       }

&#x20;   }

};

}),

&#x20;   **{"offset": {"line": 130, "column": 0}, "map": {"version":3,"sources":\["turbopack:///\[turbopack-node]/error.ts"],"sourcesContent":\["import type { StackFrame } from './compiled/stacktrace-parser'\\nimport { parse as parseStackTrace } from './compiled/stacktrace-parser'\\n\\n// merged from next.js\\n// https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/e657741b9908cf0044aaef959c0c4defb19ed6d8/packages/next/src/lib/is-error.ts\\n// https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/e657741b9908cf0044aaef959c0c4defb19ed6d8/packages/next/src/shared/lib/is-plain-object.ts\\n\\nexport default function isError(err: unknown): err is Error {\\n  return (\\n    typeof err === 'object' \&\& err !== null \&\& 'name' in err \&\& 'message' in err\\n  )\\n}\\n\\nexport function getProperError(err: unknown): Error {\\n  if (isError(err)) {\\n    return err\\n  }\\n\\n  if (process.env.NODE\_ENV === 'development') {\\n    // Provide a better error message for cases where `throw undefined`\\n    // is called in development\\n    if (typeof err === 'undefined') {\\n      return new Error('`undefined` was thrown instead of a real error')\\n    }\\n\\n    if (err === null) {\\n      return new Error('`null` was thrown instead of a real error')\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  return new Error(isPlainObject(err) ? JSON.stringify(err) : err + '')\\n}\\n\\nfunction getObjectClassLabel(value: any): string {\\n  return Object.prototype.toString.call(value)\\n}\\n\\nfunction isPlainObject(value: any): boolean {\\n  if (getObjectClassLabel(value) !== '\[object Object]') {\\n    return false\\n  }\\n\\n  const prototype = Object.getPrototypeOf(value)\\n\\n  /\*\*\\n   \* this used to be previously:\\n   \*\\n   \* `return prototype === null || prototype === Object.prototype`\\n   \*\\n   \* but Edge Runtime expose Object from vm, being that kind of type-checking wrongly fail.\\n   \*\\n   \* It was changed to the current implementation since it's resilient to serialization.\\n   \*/\\n  return prototype === null || prototype.hasOwnProperty('isPrototypeOf')\\n}\\n\\nexport type StructuredError = {\\n  name: string\\n  message: string\\n  stack: StackFrame\[]\\n  cause: StructuredError | undefined\\n}\\n\\nexport function structuredError(e: Error | string): StructuredError {\\n  e = getProperError(e)\\n\\n  return {\\n    name: e.name,\\n    message: e.message,\\n    stack: typeof e.stack === 'string' ? parseStackTrace(e.stack) : \[],\\n    cause: e.cause ? structuredError(getProperError(e.cause)) : undefined,\\n  }\\n}\\n"],"names":\["isError","err","getProperError","Error","isPlainObject","JSON","stringify","getObjectClassLabel","value","Object","prototype","toString","call","getPrototypeOf","hasOwnProperty","structuredError","e","name","message","stack","cause","undefined"],"mappings":";;;;;;;;AACA;;AAMe,SAASA,QAAQC,GAAY;IAC1C,OACE,OAAOA,QAAQ,YAAYA,QAAQ,QAAQ,UAAUA,OAAO,aAAaA;AAE7E;AAEO,SAASC,eAAeD,GAAY;IACzC,IAAID,QAAQC,MAAM;QAChB,OAAOA;IACT;IAEA;;IAYA,OAAO,IAAIE,MAAMC,cAAcH,OAAOI,KAAKC,SAAS,CAACL,OAAOA,MAAM;AACpE;AAEA,SAASM,oBAAoBC,KAAU;IACrC,OAAOC,OAAOC,SAAS,CAACC,QAAQ,CAACC,IAAI,CAACJ;AACxC;AAEA,SAASJ,cAAcI,KAAU;IAC/B,IAAID,oBAAoBC,WAAW,mBAAmB;QACpD,OAAO;IACT;IAEA,MAAME,YAAYD,OAAOI,cAAc,CAACL;IAExC;;;;;;;;GAQC,GACD,OAAOE,cAAc,QAAQA,UAAUI,cAAc,CAAC;AACxD;AASO,SAASC,gBAAgBC,CAAiB;IAC/CA,IAAId,eAAec;IAEnB,OAAO;QACLC,MAAMD,EAAEC,IAAI;QACZC,SAASF,EAAEE,OAAO;QAClBC,OAAO,OAAOH,EAAEG,KAAK,KAAK,WAAW,IAAA,yJAAe,EAACH,EAAEG,KAAK,IAAI,EAAE;QAClEC,OAAOJ,EAAEI,KAAK,GAAGL,gBAAgBb,eAAec,EAAEI,KAAK,KAAKC;IAC9D;AACF"}},**

&#x20;   **{"offset": {"line": 182, "column": 0}, "map": {"version":3,"sources":\["turbopack:///\[turbopack-node]/child\_process/index.ts"],"sourcesContent":\["import { createConnection } from 'node:net'\\nimport { Writable } from 'node:stream'\\nimport { structuredError } from '../error'\\n\\ntype State =\\n  | {\\n      type: 'waiting'\\n    }\\n  | {\\n      type: 'packet'\\n      length: number\\n    }\\n\\nexport type Ipc<TIncoming, TOutgoing> = {\\n  recv(): Promise<TIncoming>\\n  send(message: TOutgoing): Promise<void>\\n  sendError(error: Error | string): Promise<never>\\n  sendReady(): Promise<void>\\n}\\n\\nfunction createIpc<TIncoming, TOutgoing>(\\n  port: number\\n): Ipc<TIncoming, TOutgoing> {\\n  const socket = createConnection({\\n    port,\\n    host: '127.0.0.1',\\n  })\\n\\n  /\*\*\\n   \* A writable stream that writes to the socket.\\n   \* We don't write directly to the socket because we need to\\n   \* handle backpressure and wait for the socket to be drained\\n   \* before writing more data.\\n   \*/\\n  const socketWritable = new Writable({\\n    write(chunk, \_enc, cb) {\\n      if (socket.write(chunk)) {\\n        cb()\\n      } else {\\n        socket.once('drain', cb)\\n      }\\n    },\\n    final(cb) {\\n      socket.end(cb)\\n    },\\n  })\\n\\n  const packetQueue: Buffer\[] = \[]\\n  const recvPromiseResolveQueue: Array<(message: TIncoming) => void> = \[]\\n\\n  function pushPacket(packet: Buffer) {\\n    const recvPromiseResolve = recvPromiseResolveQueue.shift()\\n    if (recvPromiseResolve != null) {\\n      recvPromiseResolve(JSON.parse(packet.toString('utf8')) as TIncoming)\\n    } else {\\n      packetQueue.push(packet)\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  let state: State = { type: 'waiting' }\\n  let buffer: Buffer = Buffer.alloc(0)\\n  socket.once('connect', () => {\\n    socket.setNoDelay(true)\\n    socket.on('data', (chunk) => {\\n      buffer = Buffer.concat(\[buffer, chunk])\\n\\n      loop: while (true) {\\n        switch (state.type) {\\n          case 'waiting': {\\n            if (buffer.length >= 4) {\\n              const length = buffer.readUInt32BE(0)\\n              buffer = buffer.subarray(4)\\n              state = { type: 'packet', length }\\n            } else {\\n              break loop\\n            }\\n            break\\n          }\\n          case 'packet': {\\n            if (buffer.length >= state.length) {\\n              const packet = buffer.subarray(0, state.length)\\n              buffer = buffer.subarray(state.length)\\n              state = { type: 'waiting' }\\n              pushPacket(packet)\\n            } else {\\n              break loop\\n            }\\n            break\\n          }\\n          default:\\n            invariant(state, (state) => `Unknown state type: ${state?.type}`)\\n        }\\n      }\\n    })\\n  })\\n  // When the socket is closed, this process is no longer needed.\\n  // This might happen e. g. when parent process is killed or\\n  // node.js pool is garbage collected.\\n  socket.once('close', () => {\\n    process.exit(0)\\n  })\\n\\n  // TODO(lukesandberg): some of the messages being sent are very large and contain lots\\n  //  of redundant information.  Consider adding gzip compression to our stream.\\n  function doSend(message: string): Promise<void> {\\n    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\\n      // Reserve 4 bytes for our length prefix, we will over-write after encoding.\\n      const packet = Buffer.from('0000' + message, 'utf8')\\n      packet.writeUInt32BE(packet.length - 4, 0)\\n      socketWritable.write(packet, (err) => {\\n        process.stderr.write(`TURBOPACK\_OUTPUT\_D\\\\n`)\\n        process.stdout.write(`TURBOPACK\_OUTPUT\_D\\\\n`)\\n        if (err != null) {\\n          reject(err)\\n        } else {\\n          resolve()\\n        }\\n      })\\n    })\\n  }\\n\\n  function send(message: any): Promise<void> {\\n    return doSend(JSON.stringify(message))\\n  }\\n  function sendReady(): Promise<void> {\\n    return doSend('')\\n  }\\n\\n  return {\\n    async recv() {\\n      const packet = packetQueue.shift()\\n      if (packet != null) {\\n        return JSON.parse(packet.toString('utf8')) as TIncoming\\n      }\\n\\n      const result = await new Promise<TIncoming>((resolve) => {\\n        recvPromiseResolveQueue.push((result) => {\\n          resolve(result)\\n        })\\n      })\\n\\n      return result\\n    },\\n\\n    send(message: TOutgoing) {\\n      return send(message)\\n    },\\n\\n    sendReady,\\n\\n    async sendError(error: Error): Promise<never> {\\n      let failed = false\\n      try {\\n        await send({\\n          type: 'error',\\n          ...structuredError(error),\\n        })\\n      } catch (err) {\\n        // There's nothing we can do about errors that happen after this point, we can't tell anyone\\n        // about them.\\n        console.error('failed to send error back to rust:', err)\\n        failed = true\\n      }\\n      await new Promise<void>((res) => socket.end(() => res()))\\n      process.exit(failed ? 1 : 0)\\n    },\\n  }\\n}\\n\\nconst PORT = process.argv\[2]\\n\\nexport const IPC = createIpc<unknown, './index'\\nimport type { Ipc as GenericIpc } from './index'\\n\\nimport type { Channel as Ipc } from '../types'\\n\\ntype IpcIncomingMessage =\\n  | {\\n      type: 'evaluate'\\n      args: string\[]\\n    }\\n  | {\\n      type: 'result'\\n      id: number\\n      error: string | null\\n      data: any | null\\n    }\\n\\ntype IpcOutgoingMessage =\\n  | {\\n      type: 'end'\\n      data: string | undefined\\n      duration: number\\n    }\\n  | {\\n      type: 'info'\\n      data: any\\n    }\\n  | {\\n      type: 'request'\\n      id: number\\n      data: any\\n    }\\n\\nconst ipc = IPC as GenericIpc<IpcIncomingMessage, IpcOutgoingMessage>\\n\\nconst queue: string\[]\[] = \[]\\n\\nexport const run = async (\\n  moduleFactory: () => Promise<{\\n    init?: () => Promise<void>\\n    default: (ipc: Ipc<any, any>, ...deserializedArgs: any\[]) => any\\n  }>\\n) => {\\n  let nextId = 1\\n  const requests = new Map()\\n  const internalIpc = {\\n    sendInfo: (message: any) =>\\n      ipc.send({\\n        type: 'info',\\n        data: message,\\n      }),\\n    sendRequest: (message: any) => {\\n      const id = nextId++\\n      let resolve, reject\\n      const promise = new Promise((res, rej) => {\\n        resolve = res\\n        reject = rej\\n      })\\n      requests.set(id, { resolve, reject })\\n      return ipc\\n        .send({ type: 'request', id, data: message })\\n        .then(() => promise)\\n    },\\n    sendError: (error: Error) => {\\n      return ipc.sendError(error)\\n    },\\n  }\\n\\n  // Initialize module and send ready message\\n  let getValue: (ipc: Ipc<any, any>, ...deserializedArgs: any\[]) => any\\n  try {\\n    const module = await moduleFactory()\\n    if (typeof module.init === 'function') {\\n      await module.init()\\n    }\\n    getValue = module.default\\n    await ipc.sendReady()\\n  } catch (err) {\\n    await ipc.sendReady()\\n    await ipc.sendError(err as Error)\\n  }\\n\\n  // Queue handling\\n  let isRunning = false\\n  const run = async () => {\\n    while (queue.length > 0) {\\n      const args = queue.shift()!\\n      try {\\n        const value = await getValue(internalIpc, ...args)\\n        await ipc.send({\\n          type: 'end',\\n          data:\\n            value === undefined ? undefined : JSON.stringify(value, null, 2),\\n          duration: 0,\\n        })\\n      } catch (e) {\\n        await ipc.sendError(e as Error)\\n      }\\n    }\\n    isRunning = false\\n  }\\n\\n  // Communication handling\\n  while (true) {\\n    const msg = await ipc.recv()\\n\\n    switch (msg.type) {\\n      case 'evaluate': {\\n        queue.push(msg.args)\\n        if (!isRunning) {\\n          isRunning = true\\n          run()\\n        }\\n        break\\n      }\\n Error(msg.error))\\n          } else {\\n            request.resolve(msg.data)\\n          }\\n        }\\n        break\\n      }\\n      default: {\\n        console.error('unexpected message type', (msg as any).type)\\n        process.exit(1)\\n      }\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n\\nexport type { IpcIncomingMessage, IpcOutgoingMessage **const RUNTIME\_PUBLIC\_PATH = "chunks/\[turbopack]\_runtime.js";**

**const RELATIVE\_ROOT\_PATH = "..";**

**const ASSET\_PREFIX = "/";**

**const WORKER\_FORWARDED\_GLOBALS = \[];**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* This file contains runtime types and functions that are shared between all**

&#x20;**\* TurboPack ECMAScript runtimes.**

&#x20;**\***

&#x20;**\* It will be prepended to the runtime code of each runtime.**

&#x20;**\*/ /\* eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars \*/ /// <reference path="./runtime-types.d.ts" />**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Describes why a module was instantiated.**

&#x20;**\* Shared between browser and Node.js runtimes.**

&#x20;**\*/ var SourceType = /\*#\_\_PURE\_\_\*/ function(SourceType) {**

&#x20;   **/\*\***

&#x20;  **\* The module was instantiated because it was included in an evaluated chunk's**

&#x20;  **\* runtime.**

&#x20;  **\* SourceData is a ChunkPath.**

&#x20;  **\*/ SourceType\[SourceType\["Runtime"] = 0] = "Runtime";**

&#x20;   **/\*\***

&#x20;  **\* The module was instantiated because a parent module imported it.**

&#x20;  **\* SourceData is a ModuleId.**

&#x20;  **\*/ SourceType\[SourceType\["Parent"] = 1] = "Parent";**

&#x20;   **/\*\***

&#x20;  **\* The module was instantiated because it was included in a chunk's hot module**

&#x20;  **\* update.**

&#x20;  **\* SourceData is an array of ModuleIds or undefined.**

&#x20;  **\*/ SourceType\[SourceType\["Update"] = 2] = "Update";**

&#x20;   **return SourceType;**

**}(SourceType || {});**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Flag indicating which module object type to create when a module is merged. Set to `true`**

&#x20;**\* by each runtime that uses ModuleWithDirection (browser dev-base.ts, nodejs dev-base.ts,**

&#x20;**\* nodejs build-base.ts). Browser production (build-base.ts) leaves it as `false` since it**

&#x20;**\* uses plain Module objects.**

&#x20;**\*/ let createModuleWithDirectionFlag = false;**

**const REEXPORTED\_OBJECTS = new WeakMap();**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Constructs the `\_\_turbopack\_context\_\_` object for a module.**

&#x20;**\*/ function Context(module, exports) {**

&#x20;   **this.m = module;**

&#x20;   **// We need to store this here instead of accessing it from the module object to:**

&#x20;   **// 1. Make it available to factories directly, since we rewrite `this` to**

&#x20;   **//    `\_\_turbopack\_context\_\_.e` in CJS modules.**

&#x20;   **// 2. Support async modules which rewrite `module.exports` to a promise, so we**

&#x20;   **//    can still access the original exports object from functions like**

&#x20;   **//    `esmExport`**

&#x20;   **// Ideally we could find a new approach for async modules and drop this property altogether.**

&#x20;   **this.e = exports;**

**}**

**const contextPrototype = Context.prototype;**

**const hasOwnProperty = Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty;**

**const toStringTag = typeof Symbol !== 'undefined' \&\& Symbol.toStringTag;**

**function defineProp(obj, name, options) {**

&#x20;   **if (!hasOwnProperty.call(obj, name)) Object.defineProperty(obj, name, options);**

**}**

**function getOverwrittenModule(moduleCache, id) {**

&#x20;   **let module = moduleCache\[id];**

&#x20;   **if (!module) {**

&#x20;       **if (createModuleWithDirectionFlag) {**

&#x20;           **// set in development modes for hmr support**

&#x20;           **module = createModuleWithDirection(id);**

&#x20;       **} else {**

&#x20;           **module = createModuleObject(id);**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;       **moduleCache\[id] = module;**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **return module;**

**}**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Creates the module object. Only done here to ensure all module objects have the same shape.**

&#x20;**\*/ function createModuleObject(id) {**

&#x20;   **return {**

&#x20;       **exports: {},**

&#x20;       **error: undefined,**

&#x20;       **id,**

&#x20;       **namespaceObject: undefined**

&#x20;   **};**

**}**

**function createModuleWithDirection(id) {**

&#x20;       **error: undefined,**

&#x20;       **id,**

&#x20;       **namespaceObject: undefined,**

&#x20;       **parents: \[],**

&#x20;       **children: \[]**

&#x20;   **};**

**}**

**const BindingTag\_Value = 0;**

**/\*\***

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* A pseudo "fake" URL object to resolve to its relative path.**

&#x20;**\* When UrlRewriteBehavior is set to relative, calls to the `new URL()` will construct url without base using this**

&#x20;**\* This is based on webpack's existing implementation:**

&#x20;       **enumerable: true,**

&#x20;   **switch(sourceType){**

&#x20;       **case 1:**

&#x20;   **{"offset": {"line": 580, "column": 0}, "map": {"version":3,"sources":\["turbopack:///\[turbopack]/shared-node/base-externals-utils.ts"],"sourcesContent":\["/// <reference path=\\"../shared/runtime/runtime-utils.ts\\" />\\n\\n/// A 'base' utilities to support runtime can have externals.\\n/// Currently this is for node.js / edge runtime both.\\n/// If a fn requires node.js specific behavior, it should be placed in `node-external-utils` instead.\\n\\nasync function externalImport(id: DependencySpecifier) {\\n  let raw\\n  try {\\n    raw = await import(id)\\n  } catch (err) {\\n    // TODO(alexkirsz) This can happen when a client-side module tries to load\\n    // an external module we don't provide a shim for (e.g. querystring, url).\\n    // For now, we fail semi-silently, but in the future this should be a\\n    // compilation error.\\n    throw new Error(`Failed to load external module ${id}: ${err}`)\\n  }\\n\\n  if (raw \&\& raw.\_\_esModule \&\& raw.default \&\& 'default' in raw.default) {\\n    return interopEsm(raw.default, createNS(raw), true)\\n  }\\n\\n  return raw\\n}\\ncontextPrototype.y = externalImport\\n\\nfunction externalRequire(\\n  id: ModuleId,\\n  thunk: () => any,\\n  esm: boolean = false\\n): Exports | EsmNamespaceObject {\\n  let raw\\n  try {\\n    raw = thunk()\\n  } catch (err) {\\n    // TODO(alexkirsz) This can happen when a client-side module tries to load\\n    // an external module we don't provide a shim for (e.g. querystring, url).\\n    // For now, we fail semi-silently, but in the future this should be a\\n    // compilation error.\\n    throw new Error(`Failed to load external module ${id}: ${err}`)\\n  }\\n\\n  if (!esm || raw.\_\_esModule) {\\n    return raw\\n  }\\n\\n  return interopEsm(raw, createNS(raw), true)\\n}\\n\\nexternalRequire.resolve = (\\n  id: string,\\n  options?: {\\n    paths?: string\[]\\n  }\\n) => {\\n  return require.resolve(id, options)\\n}\\ncontextPrototype.x = externalRequire\\n"],"names":\["externalImport","id","raw","err","Error","\_\_esModule","default","interopEsm","createNS","contextPrototype","y","externalRequire","thunk","esm","resolve","options","require","x"],"mappings":"AAAA,2DAA2D;AAE3D,6DAA6D;AAC7D,sDAAsD;AACtD,qGAAqG;AAErG,eAAeA,eAAeC,EAAuB;IACnD,IAAIC;IACJ,IAAI;QACFA,MAAM,MAAM,MAAM,CAACD;IACrB,EAAE,OAAOE,KAAK;QACZ,0EAA0E;QAC1E,0EAA0E;QAC1E,qEAAqE;QACrE,qBAAqB;QACrB,MAAM,IAAIC,MAAM,CAAC,+BAA+B,EAAEH,GAAG,EAAE,EAAEE,KAAK;IAChE;IAEA,IAAID,OAAOA,IAAIG,UAAU,IAAIH,IAAII,OAAO,IAAI,aAAaJ,IAAII,OAAO,EAAE;QACpE,OAAOC,WAAWL,IAAII,OAAO,EAAEE,SAASN,MAAM;IAChD;IAEA,OAAOA;AACT;AACAO,iBAAiBC,CAAC,GAAGV;AAErB,SAASW,gBACPV,EAAY,EACZW,KAAgB,EAChBC,MAAe,KAAK;IAEpB,IAAIX;IACJ,IAAI;QACFA,MAAMU;IACR,EAAE,OAAOT,KAAK;QACZ,0EAA0E;QAC1E,0EAA0E;QAC1E,qEAAqE;QACrE,qBAAqB;QACrB,MAAM,IAAIC,MAAM,CAAC,+BAA+B,EAAEH,GAAG,EAAE,EAAEE,KAAK;IAChE;IAEA,IAAI,CAACU,OAAOX,IAAIG,UAAU,EAAE;QAC1B,OAAOH;IACT;IAEA,OAAOK,WAAWL,KAAKM,SAASN,MAAM;AACxC;AAEAS,gBAAgBG,OAAO,GAAG,CACxBb,IACAc;IAIA,OAAOC,QAAQF,OAAO,CAACb,IAAIc;AAC7B;AACAN,iBAAiBQ,CAAC,GAAGN","ignoreList":\[0]}},**

&#x20;   **{"offset": {"line": 621, "column": 0}, "map": {"version":3,"sources":\["turbopack:///\[turbopack]/shared-node/node-externals-utils.ts"],"sourcesContent":\["/\* eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars \*/\\n\\ndeclare var RUNTIME\_PUBLIC\_PATH: string\\ndeclare var RELATIVE\_ROOT\_PATH: string\\ndeclare var ASSET\_PREFIX: string\\n\\nconst path = require('path')\\n\\nconst relativePathToRuntimeRoot = path.relative(RUNTIME\_PUBLIC\_PATH, '.')\\n// Compute the relative path to the `distDir`.\\nconst relativePathToDistRoot = path.join(\\n  relativePathToRuntimeRoot,\\n  RELATIVE\_ROOT\_PATH\\n)\\nconst RUNTIME\_ROOT = path.resolve(\_\_filename, relativePathToRuntimeRoot)\\n// Compute the absolute path to the root, by stripping distDir from the absolute path to this file.\\nconst ABSOLUTE\_ROOT = path.resolve(\_\_filename, relativePathToDistRoot)\\n\\n/\*\*\\n \* Returns an absolute path to the given module path.\\n \* Module path should be relative, either path to a file or a directory.\\n \*\\n \* This fn allows to calculate an absolute path for some global static values, such as\\n \* `\_\_dirname` or `import.meta.url` that Turbopack will not embeds in compile time.\\n \* See ImportMetaBinding::code\_generation for the usage.\\n \*/\\nfunction resolveAbsolutePath(modulePath?: string): string {\\n  if (modulePath) {\\n    return path.join(ABSOLUTE\_ROOT, modulePath)\\n  }\\n  return ABSOLUTE\_ROOT\\n}\\nContext.prototype.P = resolveAbsolutePath\\n"],"names":\["path","require","relativePathToRuntimeRoot","relative","RUNTIME\_PUBLIC\_PATH","relativePathToDistRoot","join","RELATIVE\_ROOT\_PATH","RUNTIME\_ROOT","resolve","\_\_filename","ABSOLUTE\_ROOT","resolveAbsolutePath","modulePath","Context","prototype","P"],"mappings":"AAAA,oDAAoD,GAMpD,MAAMA,OAAOC,QAAQ;AAErB,MAAMC,4BAA4BF,KAAKG,QAAQ,CAACC,qBAAqB;AACrE,8CAA8C;AAC9C,MAAMC,yBAAyBL,KAAKM,IAAI,CACtCJ,2BACAK;AAEF,MAAMC,eAAeR,KAAKS,OAAO,CAACC,YAAYR;AAC9C,mGAAmG;AACnG,MAAMS,gBAAgBX,KAAKS,OAAO,CAACC,YAAYL;AAE/C;;;;;;;CAOC,GACD,SAASO,oBAAoBC,UAAmB;IAC9C,IAAIA,YAAY;QACd,OAAOb,KAAKM,IAAI,CAACK,eAAeE;IAClC;IACA,OAAOF;AACT;AACAG,QAAQC,SAAS,CAACC,CAAC,GAAGJ","ignoreList":\[0]}},**

&#x20;   **{"offset": {"line": 642, "column": 0}, "map": {"version":3,"sources":\["turbopack:///\[turbopack]/shared-node/node-wasm-utils.ts"],"sourcesContent":\["/\* eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars \*/\\n\\n/// <reference path=\\"../shared/runtime/runtime-utils.ts\\" />\\n\\nfunction readWebAssemblyAsResponse(path: string) {\\n  const { createReadStream } = require('fs') as typeof import('fs')\\n  const { Readable } = require('stream') as typeof import('stream')\\n\\n  const stream = createReadStream(path)\\n\\n  // @ts-ignore unfortunately there's a slight type mismatch with the stream.\\n  return new Response(Readable.toWeb(stream), {\\n    headers: {\\n      'content-type': 'application/wasm',\\n    },\\n  })\\n}\\n\\nasync function compileWebAssemblyFromPath(\\n  path: string\\n): Promise<WebAssembly.Module> {\\n  const response = readWebAssemblyAsResponse(path)\\n\\n  return await WebAssembly.compileStreaming(response)\\n}\\n\\nasync function instantiateWebAssemblyFromPath(\\n  path: string,\\n  importsObj: WebAssembly.Imports\\n): Promise<Exports> {\\n  const response = readWebAssemblyAsResponse(path)\\n\\n  const { instance } = await WebAssembly.instantiateStreaming(\\n    response,\\n    importsObj\\n  )\\n\\n  return instance.exports\\n}\\n"],"names":\["readWebAssemblyAsResponse","path","createReadStream","require","Readable","stream","Response","toWeb","headers","compileWebAssemblyFromPath","response","WebAssembly","compileStreaming","instantiateWebAssemblyFromPath","importsObj","instance","instantiateStreaming","exports"],"mappings":"AAAA,oDAAoD,GAEpD,2DAA2D;AAE3D,SAASA,0BAA0BC,IAAY;IAC7C,MAAM,EAAEC,gBAAgB,EAAE,GAAGC,QAAQ;IACrC,MAAM,EAAEC,QAAQ,EAAE,GAAGD,QAAQ;IAE7B,MAAME,SAASH,iBAAiBD;IAEhC,2EAA2E;IAC3E,OAAO,IAAIK,SAASF,SAASG,KAAK,CAACF,SAAS;QAC1CG,SAAS;YACP,gBAAgB;QAClB;IACF;AACF;AAEA,eAAeC,2BACbR,IAAY;IAEZ,MAAMS,WAAWV,0BAA0BC;IAE3C,OAAO,MAAMU,YAAYC,gBAAgB,CAACF;AAC5C;AAEA,eAAeG,+BACbZ,IAAY,EACZa,UAA+B;IAE/B,MAAMJ,WAAWV,0BAA0BC;IAE3C,MAAM,EAAEc,QAAQ,EAAE,GAAG,MAAMJ,YAAYK,oBAAoB,CACzDN,UACAI;IAGF,OAAOC,SAASE,OAAO;AACzB","ignoreList":\[0]}},**

&#x20;   **{"offset": {"line": 663, "column": 0}, "map": {"version":3,"sources":\["turbopack:///\[turbopack]/nodejs/runtime/runtime-base.ts"],"sourcesContent":\["/\* eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars \*/\\n\\n/// <reference path=\\"../../shared/runtime/runtime-utils.ts\\" />\\n/// <reference path=\\"../../shared-node/base-externals-utils.ts\\" />\\n/// <reference path=\\"../../shared-node/node-externals-utils.ts\\" />\\n/// <reference path=\\"../../shared-node/node-wasm-utils.ts\\" />\\n/// <reference path=\\"./nodejs-globals.d.ts\\" />\\n\\n/\*\*\\n \* Base Node.js runtime shared between production and development.\\n \* Contains chunk loading, module caching, and other non-HMR functionality.\\n \*/\\n\\nprocess.env.TURBOPACK = '1'\\n\\nconst url = require('url') as typeof import('url')\\n\\nconst moduleFactories: ModuleFactories = new Map()\\nconst moduleCache: ModuleCache<Module> = Object.create(null)\\n\\n/\*\*\\n \* Returns an absolute path to the given module's id.\\n \*/\\nfunction resolvePathFromModule(\\n  this: TurbopackBaseContext<Module>,\\n  moduleId: string\\n): string {\\n  const exported = this.r(moduleId)\\n  const exportedPath = exported?.default ?? exported\\n  if (typeof exportedPath !== 'string') {\\n    return exported as any\\n  }\\n\\n  const strippedAssetPrefix = exportedPath.slice(ASSET\_PREFIX.length)\\n  const resolved = path.resolve(RUNTIME\_ROOT, strippedAssetPrefix)\\n\\n  return url.pathToFileURL(resolved).href\\n}\\n\\n/\*\*\\n \* Exports a URL value. No suffix is added in Node.js runtime.\\n \*/\\nfunction exportUrl(\\n  this: TurbopackBaseContext<Module>,\\n  urlValue: string,\\n  id: ModuleId | undefined\\n) {\\n  exportValue.call(this, urlValue, id)\\n}\\n\\nfunction loadRuntimeChunk(sourcePath: ChunkPath, chunkData: ChunkData): void {\\n  if (typeof chunkData === 'string') {\\n    loadRuntimeChunkPath(sourcePath, chunkData)\\n  } else {\\n    loadRuntimeChunkPath(sourcePath, chunkData.path)\\n  }\\n}\\n\\nconst loadedChunks = new Set<ChunkPath>()\\nconst unsupportedLoadChunk = Promise.resolve(undefined)\\nconst loadedChunk: Promise<void> = Promise.resolve(undefined)\\nconst chunkCache = new Map<ChunkPath, Promise<void>>()\\n\\nfunction clearChunkCache() {\\n  chunkCache.clear()\\n  loadedChunks.clear()\\n}\\n\\nfunction **let p = process || {}, argv = p.argv || \[], env = p.env || {};**

**let isColorSupported = !(!!env.NO\_COLOR || argv.includes("--no-color")) \&\& (!!env.FORCE\_COLOR || argv.includes("--color") || p.platform === "win32" || (p.stdout || {}).isTTY \&\& env.TERM !== "dumb" || !!env.CI);**

**let formatter = (open, close, replace = open)=>(input)=>{**

&#x20;       **let string = "" + input, index = string.indexOf(close, open.length);**

&#x20;       **return \~index ? open + replaceClose(string, close, replace, index) + close : open + string + close;**

&#x20;   **};**

**let replaceClose = (string, close, replace, index)=>{**

&#x20;   **let result = "", cursor = 0;**

&#x20;   **do {**

&#x20;       **result += string.substring(cursor, index) + replace;**

&#x20;       **cursor = index + close.length;**

&#x20;       **index = string.indexOf(close, cursor);**

&#x20;   **}while (\~index)**

&#x20;   **return result + string.substring(cursor);**

**};**

**let createColors = (enabled = isColorSupported)=>{**

&#x20;   **let f = enabled ? formatter : ()=>String;**

&#x20;   **return {**

&#x20;       **isColorSupported: enabled,**

&#x20;       **reset: f("\\x1b\[0m", "\\x1b\[0m"),**

&#x20;       **bold: f("\\x1b\[1m", "\\x1b\[22m", "\\x1b\[22m\\x1b\[1m"),**

&#x20;       **dim: f("\\x1b\[2m", "\\x1b\[22m", "\\x1b\[22m\\x1b\[2m"),**

&#x20;       **italic: f("\\x1b\[3m", "\\x1b\[23m"),**

&#x20;       **inverse: f("\\x1b\[7m", "\\x1b\[27m"),**

&#x20;       **hidden: f("\\x1b\[8m", "\\x1b\[28m"),**

&#x20;       **strikethrough: f("\\x1b\[9m", "\\x1b\[29m"),**

&#x20;       **black: f("\\x1b\[30m", "\\x1b\[39m"),**

&#x20;       **red: f("\\x1b\[31m", "\\x1b\[39m"),**

&#x20;       **green: f("\\x1b\[32m", "\\x1b\[39m"),**

&#x20;       **yellow: f("\\x1b\[33m", "\\x1b\[39m"),**

&#x20;       **blue: f("\\x1b\[34m", "\\x1b\[39m"),**

&#x20;       **magenta: f("\\x1b\[35m", "\\x1b\[39m"),**

&#x20;       **cyan: f("\\x1b\[36m", "\\x1b\[39m"),**

&#x20;       **white: f("\\x1b\[37m", "\\x1b\[39m"),**

&#x20;       **gray: f("\\x1b\[90m", "\\x1b\[39m"),**

&#x20;       **bgBlack: f("\\x1b\[40m", "\\x1b\[49m"),**

&#x20;       **bgRed: f("\\x1b\[41m", "\\x1b\[49m"),**

&#x20;       **bgGreen: f("\\x1b\[42m", "\\x1b\[49m"),**

&#x20;       **bgYellow: f("\\x1b\[43m", "\\x1b\[49m"),**

&#x20;       **bgBlue: f("\\x1b\[44m", "\\x1b\[49m"),**

&#x20;       **bgMagenta: f("\\x1b\[45m", "\\x1b\[49m"),**

&#x20;       **bgCyan: f("\\x1b\[46m", "\\x1b\[49m"),**

&#x20;       **bgWhite: f("\\x1b\[47m", "\\x1b\[49m"),**

&#x20;       **blackBright: f("\\x1b\[90m", "\\x1b\[39m"),**

&#x20;       **redBright: f("\\x1b\[91m", "\\x1b\[39m"),**

&#x20;       **greenBright: f("\\x1b\[92m", "\\x1b\[39m"),**

&#x20;       **yellowBright: f("\\x1b\[93m", "\\x1b\[39m"),**

&#x20;       **blueBright: f("\\x1b\[94m", "\\x1b\[39m"),**

&#x20;           **// `| 0` is more compact and faster than `Math.floor()`.**

&#x20;           **id += alphabet\[Math.random() \* alphabet.length | 0];**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;       **return id;**

&#x20;   **};**

**};**

**"\[project]/node\_modules/source-map-js/lib/binary-search.js \[postcss] (ecmascript)", ((\_\_turbopack\_context\_\_, module, exports) => {**



**/\* -\*- Mode: js; js-indent-level: 2; -\*- \*/ /\***

&#x20;**\* Copyright 2011 Mozilla Foundation and contributors**

&#x20;**\* Licensed under the New BSD license. See LICENSE or:**

&#x20;**\* http://opensource.org/licenses/BSD-3-Clause**

&#x20;**\*/ exports.GREATEST\_LOWER\_BOUND = 1;**

**exports.LEAST\_UPPER\_BOUND = 2;**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Recursive implementation of binary search.**

&#x20;**\***

&#x20;**\* @param aLow Indices here and lower do not contain the needle.**

&#x20;**\* @param aHigh Indices here and higher do not contain the needle.**

&#x20;**\* @param aNeedle The element being searched for.**

&#x20;**\* @param aHaystack The non-empty array being searched.**

&#x20;**\* @param aCompare Function which takes two elements and returns -1, 0, or 1.**

&#x20;**\* @param aBias Either 'binarySearch.GREATEST\_LOWER\_BOUND' or**

&#x20;**\*     'binarySearch.LEAST\_UPPER\_BOUND'. Specifies whether to return the**

&#x20;**\*     closest element that is smaller than or greater than the one we are**

&#x20;**\*     searching for, respectively, if the exact element cannot be found.**

&#x20;**\*/ function recursiveSearch(aLow, aHigh, aNeedle, aHaystack, aCompare, aBias) {**

&#x20;   **// This function terminates when one of the following is true:**

&#x20;   **//**

&#x20;   **//   1. We find the exact element we are looking for.**

&#x20;   **//**

&#x20;   **//   2. We did not find the exact element, but we can return the index of**

&#x20;   **//      the next-closest element.**

&#x20;   **//**

&#x20;   **//   3. We did not find the exact element, and there is no next-closest**

&#x20;   **//      element than the one we are searching for, so we return -1.**

&#x20;   **var mid = Math.floor((aHigh - aLow) / 2) + aLow;**

&#x20;   **var cmp = aCompare(aNeedle, aHaystack\[mid], true);**

&#x20;   **if (cmp === 0) {**

&#x20;       **// Found the element we are looking for.**

&#x20;       **return mid;**

&#x20;   **} else if (cmp > 0) {**

&#x20;       **// Our needle is greater than aHaystack\[mid].**

&#x20;       **if (aHigh - mid > 1) {**

&#x20;           **// The element is in the upper half.**

&#x20;           **return recursiveSearch(mid, aHigh, aNeedle, aHaystack, aCompare, aBias);**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;       **// The exact needle element was not found in this haystack. Determine if**

&#x20;       **// we are in termination case (3) or (2) and return the appropriate thing.**

&#x20;       **if (aBias == exports.LEAST\_UPPER\_BOUND) {**

&#x20;           **return aHigh < aHaystack.length ? aHigh : -1;**

&#x20;       **} else {**

&#x20;           **return mid;**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;   **} else {**

&#x20;       **// Our needle is less than aHaystack\[mid].**

&#x20;       **if (mid - aLow > 1) {**

&#x20;           **return recursiveSearch(aLow, mid, aNeedle, aHaystack, aCompare, aBias);**

&#x20;   **// are intended to be compressed and deduplicated, the TypeScript compiler**

&#x20;   **// sometimes generates source maps with duplicates in them. See Github issue**

&#x20;   **// #72 and bugzil.la/889492.**

&#x20;   **this.\_names = ArraySet.fromArray(names.map(String), true);**

&#x20;   **this.\_sources = ArraySet.fromArray(sources, true);**

&#x20;   **this.\_absoluteSources = this.\_sources.toArray().map(function(s) {**

&#x20;       **return util.computeSourceURL(sourceRoot, s, aSourceMapURL);**

&#x20;   **});**

&#x20;   **this.sourceRoot = sourceRoot;**

&#x20;   **this.sourcesContent = sourcesContent;**

&#x20;   **this.\_mappings = mappings;**

&#x20;   **this.\_sourceMapURL = aSourceMapURL;**

&#x20;   **this.file = file;**

**}**

**BasicSourceMapConsumer.prototype = Object.create(SourceMapConsumer.prototype);**

**BasicSourceMapConsumer.prototype.consumer = SourceMapConsumer;**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Utility function to find the index of a source.  Returns -1 if not**

&#x20;**\* found.**

&#x20;**\*/ BasicSourceMapConsumer.prototype.\_findSourceIndex = function(aSource) {**

&#x20;   **var relativeSource = aSource;**

&#x20;   **if (this.sourceRoot != null) {**

&#x20;       **relativeSource = util.relative(this.sourceRoot, relativeSource);**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **if (this.\_sources.has(relativeSource)) {**

&#x20;       **return this.\_sources.indexOf(relativeSource);**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **// Maybe aSource is an absolute URL as returned by |sources|.  In**

&#x20;   **// this case we can't simply undo the transform.**

&#x20;   **var i;**

&#x20;   **for(i = 0; i < this.\_absoluteSources.length; ++i){**

&#x20;       **if (this.\_absoluteSources\[i] == aSource) {**

&#x20;           **return i;**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **return -1;**

**};**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Create a BasicSourceMapConsumer from a SourceMapGenerator.**

&#x20;**\***

&#x20;**\* @param SourceMapGenerator aSourceMap**

&#x20;**\*        The source map that will be consumed.**

&#x20;**\* @param String aSourceMapURL**

&#x20;**\*        The URL at which the source map can be found (optional)**

&#x20;**\* @returns BasicSourceMapConsumer**

&#x20;**\*/ BasicSourceMapConsumer.fromSourceMap = function SourceMapConsumer\_fromSourceMap(aSourceMap, aSourceMapURL) {**

&#x20;   **var smc = Object.create(BasicSourceMapConsumer.prototype);**

&#x20;   **var names = smc.\_names = ArraySet.fromArray(aSourceMap.\_names.toArray(), true);**

&#x20;   **var sources = smc.\_sources = ArraySet.fromArray(aSourceMap.\_sources.toArray(), true);**

&#x20;   **smc.sourceRoot = aSourceMap.\_sourceRoot;**

&#x20;   **smc.sourcesContent = aSourceMap.\_generateSourcesContent(smc.\_sources.toArray(), smc.sourceRoot);**

&#x20;   **smc.file = aSourceMap.\_file;**

&#x20;   **smc.\_sourceMapURL = aSourceMapURL;**

&#x20;   **smc.\_absoluteSources = smc.\_sources.toArray().map(function(s) {**

&#x20;       **return util.computeSourceURL(smc.sourceRoot, s, aSourceMapURL);**

&#x20;   **});**

&#x20;   **// Because we are modifying the entries (by converting string sources and**

&#x20;   **// names to indices into the sources and names ArraySets), we have to make**

&#x20;   **// a copy of the entry or else bad things happen. Shared mutable state**

&#x20;   **// strikes again! See github issue #191.**

&#x20;   **var generatedMappings = aSourceMap.\_mappings.toArray().slice();**

&#x20;   **var destGeneratedMappings = smc.\_\_generatedMappings = \[];**

&#x20;   **var destOriginalMappings = smc.\_\_originalMappings = \[];**

&#x20;   **for(var i = 0, length = generatedMappings.length; i < length; i++){**

&#x20;       **var srcMapping = generatedMappings\[i];**

&#x20;       **var destMapping = new Mapping;**

&#x20;       **destMapping.generatedLine = srcMapping.generatedLine;**

&#x20;       **destMapping.generatedColumn = srcMapping.generatedColumn;**

&#x20;       **if (srcMapping.source) {**

&#x20;           **destMapping.source = sources.indexOf(srcMapping.source);**

&#x20;           **destMapping.originalLine = srcMapping.originalLine;**

&#x20;           **destMapping.originalColumn = srcMapping.originalColumn;**

&#x20;           **if (srcMapping.name) {**

&#x20;               **destMapping.name = names.indexOf(srcMapping.name);**

&#x20;           **}**

&#x20;           **destOriginalMappings.push(destMapping);**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;       **destGeneratedMappings.push(destMapping);**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **quickSort(smc.\_\_originalMappings, util.compareByOriginalPositions);**

&#x20;   **return smc;**

**};**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* The version of the source mapping spec that we are consuming.**

&#x20;**\*/ BasicSourceMapConsumer.prototype.\_version = 3;**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* The list of original sources.**

&#x20;**\*/ Object.defineProperty(BasicSourceMapConsumer.prototype, 'sources', {**

&#x20;   **get: function() {**

&#x20;       **return this.\_absoluteSources.slice();**

&#x20;   **}**

**});**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Provide the JIT with a nice shape / hidden class.**

&#x20;**\*/ function Mapping() {**

&#x20;   **this.generatedLine = 0;**

&#x20;   **this.generatedColumn = 0;**

&#x20;   **this.source = null;**

&#x20;   **this.originalLine = null;**

&#x20;   **this.originalColumn = null;**

&#x20;   **this.name = null;**

**}**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Parse the mappings in a string in to a data structure which we can easily**

&#x20;**\* query (the ordered arrays in the `this.\_\_generatedMappings` and**

&#x20;**\* `this.\_\_originalMappings` properties).**

&#x20;**\*/ const compareGenerated = util.compareByGeneratedPositionsDeflatedNoLine;**

**function sortGenerated(array, start) {**

&#x20;   **let l = array.length;**

&#x20;   **let n = array.length - start;**

&#x20;   **if (n <= 1) {**

&#x20;       **return;**

&#x20;   **} else if (n == 2) {**

&#x20;       **let a = array\[start];**

&#x20;       **let b = array\[start + 1];**

&#x20;       **if (compareGenerated(a, b) > 0) {**

&#x20;           **array\[start] = b;**

&#x20;           **array\[start + 1] = a;**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;   **} else if (n < 20) {**

&#x20;       **for(let i = start; i < l; i++){**

&#x20;           **for(let j = i; j > start; j--){**

&#x20;               **let a = array\[j - 1];**

&#x20;               **let b = array\[j];**

&#x20;               **if (compareGenerated(a, b) <= 0) {**

&#x20;                   **break;**

&#x20;               **}**

&#x20;               **array\[j - 1] = b;**

&#x20;               **array\[j] = a;**

&#x20;           **}**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;   **} else {**

&#x20;       **quickSort(array, compareGenerated, start);**

&#x20;   **}**

**}**

**BasicSourceMapConsumer.prototype.\_parseMappings = function SourceMapConsumer\_parseMappings(aStr, aSourceRoot) {**

&#x20;   **var generatedLine = 1;**

&#x20;   **var previousGeneratedColumn = 0;**

&#x20;   **var previousOriginalLine = 0;**

&#x20;   **var previousOriginalColumn = 0;**

&#x20;   **var previousSource = 0;**

&#x20;   **var previousName = 0;**

&#x20;   **var length = aStr.length;**

&#x20;   **var index = 0;**

&#x20;   **var cachedSegments = {};**

&#x20;   **var temp = {};**

&#x20;   **var originalMappings = \[];**

&#x20;   **var generatedMappings = \[];**

&#x20;   **var mapping, str, segment, end, value;**

&#x20;   **let subarrayStart = 0;**

&#x20;   **while(index < length){**

&#x20;       **if (aStr.charAt(index) === ';') {**

&#x20;           **generatedLine++;**

&#x20;           **index++;**

&#x20;           **previousGeneratedColumn = 0;**

&#x20;           **sortGenerated(generatedMappings, subarrayStart);**

&#x20;           **subarrayStart = generatedMappings.length;**

&#x20;       **} else if (aStr.charAt(index) === ',') {**

&#x20;           **index++;**

&#x20;       **} else {**

&#x20;           **mapping = new Mapping();**

&#x20;           **mapping.generatedLine = generatedLine;**

&#x20;           **for(end = index; end < length; end++){**

&#x20;               **if (this.\_charIsMappingSeparator(aStr, end)) {**

&#x20;                   **break;**

&#x20;               **}**

&#x20;           **}**

&#x20;           **str = aStr.slice(index, end);**

&#x20;           **segment = \[];**

&#x20;           **while(index < end){**

&#x20;               **base64VLQ.decode(aStr, index, temp);**

&#x20;               **value = temp.value;**

&#x20;               **index = temp.rest;**

&#x20;               **segment.push(value);**

&#x20;           **}**

&#x20;           **if (segment.length === 2) {**

&#x20;               **throw new Error('Found a source, but no line and column');**

&#x20;           **}**

&#x20;           **if (segment.length === 3) {**

&#x20;               **throw new Error('Found a source and line, but no column');**

&#x20;           **}**

&#x20;           **// Generated column.**

&#x20;           **mapping.generatedColumn = previousGeneratedColumn + segment\[0];**

&#x20;           **previousGeneratedColumn = mapping.generatedColumn;**

&#x20;           **if (segment.length > 1) {**

&#x20;               **// Original source.**

&#x20;               **mapping.source = previousSource + segment\[1];**

&#x20;               **previousSource += segment\[1];**

&#x20;               **// Original line.**

&#x20;               **mapping.originalLine = previousOriginalLine + segment\[2];**

&#x20;               **previousOriginalLine = mapping.originalLine;**

&#x20;               **// Lines are stored 0-based**

&#x20;               **mapping.originalLine += 1;**

&#x20;               **// Original column.**

&#x20;               **mapping.originalColumn = previousOriginalColumn + segment\[3];**

&#x20;               **previousOriginalColumn = mapping.originalColumn;**

&#x20;               **if (segment.length > 4) {**

&#x20;                   **// Original name.**

&#x20;                   **mapping.name = previousName + segment\[4];**

&#x20;                   **previousName += segment\[4];**

&#x20;               **}**

&#x20;           **}**

&#x20;           **generatedMappings.push(mapping);**

&#x20;           **if (typeof mapping.originalLine === 'number') {**

&#x20;               **let currentSource = mapping.source;**

&#x20;               **while(originalMappings.length <= currentSource){**

&#x20;                   **originalMappings.push(null);**

&#x20;               **}**

&#x20;               **if (originalMappings\[currentSource] === null) {**

&#x20;                   **originalMappings\[currentSource] = \[];**

&#x20;               **}**

&#x20;               **originalMappings\[currentSource].push(mapping);**

&#x20;           **}**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **sortGenerated(generatedMappings, subarrayStart);**

&#x20;   **this.\_\_generatedMappings = generatedMappings;**

&#x20;   **for(var i = 0; i < originalMappings.length; i++){**

&#x20;       **if (originalMappings\[i] != null) {**

&#x20;           **quickSort(originalMappings\[i], util.compareByOriginalPositionsNoSource);**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **this.\_\_originalMappings = \[].concat(...originalMappings);**

**};**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Find the mapping that best matches the hypothetical "needle" mapping that**

&#x20;**\* we are searching for in the given "haystack" of mappings.**

&#x20;**\*/ BasicSourceMapConsumer.prototype.\_findMapping = function SourceMapConsumer\_findMapping(aNeedle, aMappings, aLineName, aColumnName, aComparator, aBias) {**

&#x20;   **// To return the position we are searching for, we must first find the**

&#x20;   **// mapping for the given position and then return the opposite position it**

&#x20;   **// points to. Because the mappings are sorted, we can use binary search to**

&#x20;   **// find the best mapping.**

&#x20;   **if (aNeedle\[aLineName] <= 0) {**

&#x20;       **throw new TypeError('Line must be greater than or equal to 1, got ' + aNeedle\[aLineName]);**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **if (aNeedle\[aColumnName] < 0) {**

&#x20;       **throw new TypeError('Column must be greater than or equal to 0, got ' + aNeedle\[aColumnName]);**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **return binarySearch.search(aNeedle, aMappings, aComparator, aBias);**

**};**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Compute the last column for each generated mapping. The last column is**

&#x20;**\* inclusive.**

&#x20;**\*/ BasicSourceMapConsumer.prototype.computeColumnSpans = function SourceMapConsumer\_computeColumnSpans() {**

&#x20;   **for(var index = 0; index < this.\_generatedMappings.length; ++index){**

&#x20;       **var mapping = this.\_generatedMappings\[index];**

&#x20;       **// Mappings do not contain a field for the last generated columnt. We**

&#x20;       **// can come up with an optimistic estimate, however, by assuming that**

&#x20;       **// mappings are contiguous (i.e. given two consecutive mappings, the**

&#x20;       **// first mapping ends where the second one starts).**

&#x20;       **if (index + 1 < this.\_generatedMappings.length) {**

&#x20;           **var nextMapping = this.\_generatedMappings\[index + 1];**

&#x20;           **if (mapping.generatedLine === nextMapping.generatedLine) {**

&#x20;               **mapping.lastGeneratedColumn = nextMapping.generatedColumn - 1;**

&#x20;               **continue;**

&#x20;           **}**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;       **// The last mapping for each line spans the entire line.**

&#x20;       **mapping.lastGeneratedColumn = Infinity;**

&#x20;   **}**

**};**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Returns the original source, line, and column information for the generated**

&#x20;**\* source's line and column positions provided. The only argument is an object**

&#x20;**\* with the following properties:**

&#x20;**\***

&#x20;**\*   - line: The line number in the generated source.  The line number**

&#x20;**\*     is 1-based.**

&#x20;**\*   - column: The column number in the generated source.  The column**

&#x20;**\*     number is 0-based.**

&#x20;**\*   - bias: Either 'SourceMapConsumer.GREATEST\_LOWER\_BOUND' or**

&#x20;**\*     'SourceMapConsumer.LEAST\_UPPER\_BOUND'. Specifies whether to return the**

&#x20;**\*     closest element that is smaller than or greater than the one we are**

&#x20;**\*     searching for, respectively, if the exact element cannot be found.**

&#x20;**\*     Defaults to 'SourceMapConsumer.GREATEST\_LOWER\_BOUND'.**

&#x20;**\***

&#x20;**\* and an object is returned with the following properties:**

&#x20;**\***

&#x20;**\*   - source: The original source file, or null.**

&#x20;**\*   - line: The line number in the original source, or null.  The**

&#x20;**\*     line number is 1-based.**

&#x20;**\*   - column: The column number in the original source, or null.  The**

&#x20;**\*     column number is 0-based.**

&#x20;**\*   - name: The original identifier, or null.**

&#x20;**\*/ BasicSourceMapConsumer.prototype.originalPositionFor = function SourceMapConsumer\_originalPositionFor(aArgs) {**

&#x20;   **var needle = {**

&#x20;       **generatedLine: util.getArg(aArgs, 'line'),**

&#x20;       **generatedColumn: util.getArg(aArgs, 'column')**

&#x20;   **};**

&#x20;   **var index = this.\_findMapping(needle, this.\_generatedMappings, "generatedLine", "generatedColumn", util.compareByGeneratedPositionsDeflated, util.getArg(aArgs, 'bias', SourceMapConsumer.GREATEST\_LOWER\_BOUND));**

&#x20;   **if (index >= 0) {**

&#x20;       **var mapping = this.\_generatedMappings\[index];**

&#x20;       **if (mapping.generatedLine === needle.generatedLine) {**

&#x20;           **var source = util.getArg(mapping, 'source', null);**

&#x20;           **if (source !== null) {**

&#x20;               **source = this.\_sources.at(source);**

&#x20;               **source = util.computeSourceURL(this.sourceRoot, source, this.\_sourceMapURL);**

&#x20;           **}**

&#x20;           **var name = util.getArg(mapping, 'name', null);**

&#x20;           **if (name !== null) {**

&#x20;               **name = this.\_names.at(name);**

&#x20;           **}**

&#x20;           **return {**

&#x20;               **source: source,**

&#x20;               **line: util.getArg(mapping, 'originalLine', null),**

&#x20;               **column: util.getArg(mapping, 'originalColumn', null),**

&#x20;               **name: name**

&#x20;           **};**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **return {**

&#x20;       **source: null,**

&#x20;       **line: null,**

&#x20;       **column: null,**

&#x20;       **name: null**

&#x20;   **};**

**};**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Return true if we have the source content for every source in the source**

&#x20;**\* map, false otherwise.**

&#x20;**\*/ BasicSourceMapConsumer.prototype.hasContentsOfAllSources = function BasicSourceMapConsumer\_hasContentsOfAllSources() {**

&#x20;       **return false;**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **return this.sourcesContent.length >= this.\_sources.size() \&\& !this.sourcesContent.some(function(sc) {**

&#x20;       **return sc == null;**

&#x20;   **});**

**};**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Returns the original source content. The only argument is the url of the**

&#x20;**\* original source file. Returns null if no original source content is**

&#x20;**\* available.**

&#x20;**\*/ BasicSourceMapConsumer.prototype.sourceContentFor = function SourceMapConsumer\_sourceContentFor(aSource, nullOnMissing) {**

&#x20;   **if (!this.sourcesContent) {**

&#x20;       **return null;**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **var index = this.\_findSourceIndex(aSource);**

&#x20;   **if (index >= 0) {**

&#x20;       **return this.sourcesContent\[index];**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **var relativeSource = aSource;**

&#x20;   **if (this.sourceRoot != null) {**

&#x20;       **relativeSource = util.relative(this.sourceRoot, relativeSource);**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **var url;**

&#x20;   **if (this.sourceRoot != null \&\& (url = util.urlParse(this.sourceRoot))) {**

&#x20;       **// XXX: file:// URIs and absolute paths lead to unexpected behavior for**

&#x20;       **// many users. We can help them out when they expect file:// URIs to**

&#x20;       **// behave like it would if they were running a local HTTP server. See**

&#x20;       **// https://bugzilla.mozilla.org/show\_bug.cgi?id=885597.**

&#x20;       **var fileUriAbsPath = relativeSource.replace(/^file:\\/\\//, "");**

&#x20;       **if (url.scheme == "file" \&\& this.\_sources.has(fileUriAbsPath)) {**

&#x20;           **return this.sourcesContent\[this.\_sources.indexOf(fileUriAbsPath)];**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;       **if ((!url.path || url.path == "/") \&\& this.\_sources.has("/" + relativeSource)) {**

&#x20;           **return this.sourcesContent\[this.\_sources.indexOf("/" + relativeSource)];**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **// This function is used recursively from**

&#x20;   **// IndexedSourceMapConsumer.prototype.sourceContentFor. In that case, we**

&#x20;   **// don't want to throw if we can't find the source - we just want to**

&#x20;   **// return null, so we provide a flag to exit gracefully.**

&#x20;   **if (nullOnMissing) {**

&#x20;       **return null;**

&#x20;   **} else {**

&#x20;       **throw new Error('"' + relativeSource + '" is not in the SourceMap.');**

&#x20;   **}**

**};**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Returns the generated line and column information for the original source,**

&#x20;**\* line, and column positions provided. The only argument is an object with**

&#x20;**\* the following properties:**

&#x20;**\***

&#x20;**\*   - source: The filename of the original source.**

&#x20;**\*   - line: The line number in the original source.  The line number**

&#x20;**\*     is 1-based.**

&#x20;**\*   - column: The column number in the original source.  The column**

&#x20;**\*     number is 0-based.**

&#x20;**\*   - bias: Either 'SourceMapConsumer.GREATEST\_LOWER\_BOUND' or**

&#x20;**\*     'SourceMapConsumer.LEAST\_UPPER\_BOUND'. Specifies whether to return the**

&#x20;**\*     closest element that is smaller than or greater than the one we are**

&#x20;**\*     searching for, respectively, if the exact element cannot be found.**

&#x20;**\*     Defaults to 'SourceMapConsumer.GREATEST\_LOWER\_BOUND'.**

&#x20;**\***

&#x20;**\* and an object is returned with the following properties:**

&#x20;**\***

&#x20;**\*   - line: The line number in the generated source, or null.  The**

&#x20;**\*     line number is 1-based.**

&#x20;**\*   - column: The column number in the generated source, or null.**

&#x20;**\*     The column number is 0-based.**

&#x20;**\*/ BasicSourceMapConsumer.prototype.generatedPositionFor = function SourceMapConsumer\_generatedPositionFor(aArgs) {**

&#x20;   **var source = util.getArg(aArgs, 'source');**

&#x20;   **source = this.\_findSourceIndex(source);**

&#x20;   **if (source < 0) {**

&#x20;       **return {**

&#x20;           **line: null,**

&#x20;           **column: null,**

&#x20;           **lastColumn: null**

&#x20;       **};**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **var needle = {**

&#x20;       **source: source,**

&#x20;       **originalLine: util.getArg(aArgs, 'line'),**

&#x20;       **originalColumn: util.getArg(aArgs, 'column')**

&#x20;   **};**

&#x20;   **var index = this.\_findMapping(needle, this.\_originalMappings, "originalLine", "originalColumn", util.compareByOriginalPositions, util.getArg(aArgs, 'bias', SourceMapConsumer.GREATEST\_LOWER\_BOUND));**

&#x20;   **if (index >= 0) {**

&#x20;       **var mapping = this.\_originalMappings\[index];**

&#x20;       **if (mapping.source === needle.source) {**

&#x20;           **return {**

&#x20;               **line: util.getArg(mapping, 'generatedLine', null),**

&#x20;               **column: util.getArg(mapping, 'generatedColumn', null),**

&#x20;               **lastColumn: util.getArg(mapping, 'lastGeneratedColumn', null)**

&#x20;           **};**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **return {**

&#x20;       **line: null,**

&#x20;       **column: null,**

&#x20;       **lastColumn: null**

&#x20;   **};**

**};**

**exports.BasicSourceMapConsumer = BasicSourceMapConsumer;**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* An IndexedSourceMapConsumer instance represents a parsed source map which**

&#x20;**\* we can query for information. It differs from BasicSourceMapConsumer in**

&#x20;**\* that it takes "indexed" source maps (i.e. ones with a "sections" field) as**

&#x20;**\* input.**

&#x20;**\***

&#x20;**\* The first parameter is a raw source map (either as a JSON string, or already**

&#x20;**\* parsed to an object). According to the spec for indexed source maps, they**

&#x20;**\* have the following attributes:**

&#x20;**\***

&#x20;**\*   - version: Which version of the source map spec this map is following.**

&#x20;**\*   - file: Optional. The generated file this source map is associated with.**

&#x20;**\*   - sections: A list of section definitions.**

&#x20;**\***

&#x20;**\* Each value under the "sections" field has two fields:**

&#x20;**\*   - offset: The offset into the original specified at which this section**

&#x20;**\*       begins to apply, defined as an object with a "line" and "column"**

&#x20;**\*       field.**

&#x20;**\*   - map: A source map definition. This source map could also be indexed,**

&#x20;**\*       but doesn't have to be.**

&#x20;**\***

&#x20;**\* Instead of the "map" field, it's also possible to have a "url" field**

&#x20;**\* specifying a URL to retrieve a source map from, but that's currently**

&#x20;**\* unsupported.**

&#x20;**\***

&#x20;**\* Here's an example source map, taken from the source map spec\[0], but**

&#x20;**\* modified to omit a section which uses the "url" field.**

&#x20;**\***

&#x20;**\*  {**

pico = require('picocolors')\\n\\nlet tokenizer = require('./tokenize')\\n\\nlet Input\\n\\nfunction registerInput(dependant) {\\n  Input = dependant\\n}\\n\\nconst HIGHLIGHT\_THEME = {\\n  ';': pico.yellow,\\n  ':': pico.yellow,\\n  '(': pico.cyan,\\n  ')': pico.cyan,\\n  '\[': pico.yellow,\\n  ']': pico.yellow,\\n  '{': pico.yellow,\\n  '}': pico.yellow,\\n  'at-word': pico.cyan,\\n  'brackets': pico.cyan,\\n  'call': pico.cyan,\\n  'class': pico.yellow,\\n  'comment': pico.gray,\\n  'hash': pico.magenta,\\n  'string': pico.green\\n}\\n\\nfunction getTokenType(\[type, value], processor) {\\n  if (type === 'word') {\\n    if (value\[0] === '.') {\\n      return 'class'\\n    }\\n    if (value\[0] === '#') {\\n      return 'hash'\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  if (!processor.endOfFile()) {\\n    let next = processor.nextToken()\\n    processor.back(next)\\n    if (next\[0] === 'brackets' || next\[0] === '(') return 'call'\\n  }\\n\\n  return type\\n}\\n\\nfunction terminalHighlight(css) {\\n  let processor = tokenizer(new Input(css), { ignoreErrors: true })\\n  let result = ''\\n  while (!processor.endOfFile()) {\\n    let token = processor.nextToken()\\n    let color = HIGHLIGHT\_THEME\[getTokenType(token, processor)]\\n    if (color) {\\n      result += token\[1]\\n        .split(/\\\\r?\\\\n/)\\n        .map(i => color(i))\\n        .join('\\\\n')\\n    } else {\\n      result += token\[1]\\n    }\\n  }\\n  return result\\n}\\n\\nterminalHighlight.registerInput = registerInput\\n\\nmodule.exports = terminalHighlight\\n"],"names":\["pico","tokenizer","Input","registerInput","dependant","HIGHLIGHT\_THEME","yellow","cyan","gray","magenta","green","getTokenType","type","value","processor","endOfFile","next","nextToken","back","terminalHighlight","css","ignoreErrors","result","token","color","split","map","i","join","module","exports"],"mappings":"AAEA,IAAIA;AAEJ,IAAIC;AAEJ,IAAIC;AAEJ,SAASC,cAAcC,SAAS;IAC9BF,QAAQE;AACV;AAEA,MAAMC,kBAAkB;IACtB,KAAKL,KAAKM,MAAM;IAChB,KAAKN,KAAKM,MAAM;IAChB,KAAKN,KAAKO,IAAI;IACd,KAAKP,KAAKO,IAAI;IACd,KAAKP,KAAKM,MAAM;IAChB,KAAKN,KAAKM,MAAM;IAChB,KAAKN,KAAKM,MAAM;IAChB,KAAKN,KAAKM,MAAM;IAChB,WAAWN,KAAKO,IAAI;IACpB,YAAYP,KAAKO,IAAI;IACrB,QAAQP,KAAKO,IAAI;IACjB,SAASP,KAAKM,MAAM;IACpB,WAAWN,KAAKQ,IAAI;IACpB,QAAQR,KAAKS,OAAO;IACpB,UAAUT,KAAKU,KAAK;AACtB;AAEA,SAASC,aAAa,CAACC,MAAMC,MAAM,EAAEC,SAAS;IAC5C,IAAIF,SAAS,QAAQ;QACnB,IAAIC,KAAK,CAAC,EAAE,KAAK,KAAK;YACpB,OAAO;QACT;QACA,IAAIA,KAAK,CAAC,EAAE,KAAK,KAAK;YACpB,OAAO;QACT;IACF;IAEA,IAAI,CAACC,UAAUC,SAAS,IAAI;QAC1B,IAAIC,OAAOF,UAAUG,SAAS;QAC9BH,UAAUI,IAAI,CAACF;QACf,IAAIA,IAAI,CAAC,EAAE,KAAK,cAAcA,IAAI,CAAC,EAAE,KAAK,KAAK,OAAO;IACxD;IAEA,OAAOJ;AACT;AAEA,SAASO,kBAAkBC,GAAG;IAC5B,IAAIN,YAAYb,UAAU,IAAIC,MAAMkB,MAAM;QAAEC,cAAc;IAAK;IAC/D,IAAIC,SAAS;IACb,MAAO,CAACR,UAAUC,SAAS,GAAI;QAC7B,IAAIQ,QAAQT,UAAUG,SAAS;QAC/B,IAAIO,QAAQnB,eAAe,CAACM,aAAaY,OAAOT,WAAW;QAC3D,IAAIU,OAAO;YACTF,UAAUC,KAAK,CAAC,EAAE,CACfE,KAAK,CAAC,SACNC,GAAG,CAACC,CAAAA,IAAKH,MAAMG,IACfC,IAAI,CAAC;QACV,OAAO;YACLN,UAAUC,KAAK,CAAC,EAAE;QACpB;IACF;IACA,OAAOD;AACT;AAEAH,kBAAkBhB,aAAa,GAAGA;AAElC0B,OAAOC,OAAO,GAAGX","ignoreList":\[0]}},**

&#x20;   **{"offset": {"line": 405, "column": 0}, "map": {"version":3,"sources":\["turbopack:///\[project]/node\_modules/postcss/lib/css-syntax-error.js"],"sourcesContent":\["'use strict'\\n\\nlet pico = require('picocolors')\\n\\nlet terminalHighlight = require('./terminal-highlight')\\n\\nclass CssSyntaxError extends Error {\\n  constructor(message, line, column, source, file, plugin) {\\n    super(message)\\n    this.name = 'CssSyntaxError'\\n    this.reason = message\\n\\n    if (file) {\\n      this.file = file\\n    }\\n    if (source) {\\n      this.source = source\\n    }\\n    if (plugin) {\\n      this.plugin = plugin\\n    }\\n    if (typeof line !== 'undefined' \&\& typeof column !== 'undefined') {\\n      if (typeof line === 'number') {\\n        this.line = line\\n        this.column = column\\n      } else {\\n        this.line = line.line\\n        this.column = line.column\\n        this.endLine = column.line\\n        this.endColumn = column.column\\n      }\\n    }\\n\\n    this.setMessage()\\n\\n    if (Error.captureStackTrace) {\\n      Error.captureStackTrace(this, CssSyntaxError)\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  setMessage() {\\n    this.message = this.plugin ? this.plugin + ': ' : ''\\n    this.message += this.file ? this.file : '<css input>'\\n    if (typeof this.line !== 'undefined') {\\n      this.message += ':' + this.line + ':' + this.column\\n    }\\n    this.message += ': ' + this.reason\\n  }\\n\\n  showSourceCode(color) {\\n    if (!this.source) return ''\\n\\n    let css = this.source\\n    if (color == null) color = pico.isColorSupported\\n\\n    let aside = text => text\\n    let mark = text => text\\n    let highlight = text => text\\n    if (color) {\\n      let { bold, gray, red } = pico.createColors(true)\\n      mark = text => bold(red(text))\\n      aside = text => gray(text)\\n      if (terminalHighlight) {\\n        highlight = text => terminalHighlight(text)\\n      }\\n    }\\n\\n    let lines = css.split(/\\\\r?\\\\n/)\\n    let start = Math.max(this.line - 3, 0)\\n    let end = Math.min(this.line + 2, lines.length)\\n    let maxWidth = String(end).length\\n\\n    return lines\\n      .slice(start, end)\\n      .map((line, index) => {\\n        let number = start + 1 + index\\n        let gutter = ' ' + (' ' + number).slice(-maxWidth) + ' | '\\n        if (number === this.line) {\\n          if (line.length > 160) {\\n            let padding = 20\\n            let subLineStart = Math.max(0, this.column - padding)\\n            let subLineEnd = Math.max(\\n              this.column + padding,\\n              this.endColumn + padding\\n            )\\n            let subLine = line.slice(subLineStart, subLineEnd)\\n\\n            let spacing =\\n              aside(gutter.replace(/\\\\d/g, ' ')) +\\n              line\\n                .slice(0, Math.min(this.column - 1, padding - 1))\\n                .replace(/\[^\\\\t]/g, ' ')\\n\\n            return (\\n              mark('>') +\\n              aside(gutter) +\\n              highlight(subLine) +\\n              '\\\\n ' +\\n              spacing +\\n              mark('^')\\n            )\\n this.raw(node, null, 'beforeRule')\\n    } else {\\n      value = this.raw(node, null, 'beforeClose')\\n    }\\n\\n    let buf = node.parent\\n    let depth = 0\\n    while (buf \&\& buf.type !== 'root') {\\n      depth += 1\\n      buf = buf.parent\\n    }\\n\\n    if (value.includes('\\\\n')) {\\n      let indent = this.raw(node, null, 'indent')\\n      if (indent.length) {\\n        for (let step = 0; step < depth; step++) value += indent\\n      }\\n    }\\n\\n    return value\\n  }\\n\\n  block(node, start) {\\n    let between = this.raw(node, 'between', 'beforeOpen')\\n 'semicolon')\\n    let isDocument = node.type === 'document'\\n    for (let i = 0; i < nodes.length; i++) {\\n      let child = nodes\[i]\\n      let before = this.raw(child, 'before')\\n      if (before) this.builder(isDocument ? before : escapeHTMLInCSS(before))\\n      this.stringify(child, last !== i || semicolon)\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  comment(node) {\\n    let left = this.raw(node, 'left', if (value) value = value.replace(/\\\\S/g, '')\\n    return value\\n  }\\n\\n  rawColon(root) {\\n    let value\\n    root.walkDecls(i => {\\n      if (typeof i.raws.between !== 'undefined') {\\n        value = i.raws.between.replace(/\[^\\\\s:]/g, '')\\n        return false\\n      }\\n    })\\n    return value\\n  }\\n\\n  rawEmptyBody(root) {\\n    let value\\n    root.walk(i => {\\n      if (i.nodes \&\& i.nodes.length === 0) {\\n        value = i.raws.after\\n        if (typeof value !== 'undefined') return false\\n      }\\n    })\\n    return value\\n  }\\n\\n  rawIndent(root) {\\n    if (root.raws.indent) return root.raws.indent\\n    let value\\n    root.walk(i => {\\n      let p = i.parent\\n      if (p \&\& p !== root \&\& p.parent \&\& p.parent === root) {\\n        if (typeof i.raws.before !== 'undefined') {\\n          let parts = i.raws.before.split('\\\\n')\\n          value = parts\[parts.length - 1]\\n          value = value.replace(/\\\\S/g, '')\\n          return false\\n        }\\n      }\\n    })\\n    return value\\n  }\\n\\n  rawSemicolon(root) {\\n    let value\\n    root.walk(i => {\\n      if (i.nodes \&\& i.nodes.length \&\& i.last.type === 'decl') {\\n        value = i.raws.semicolon\\n        if (typeof value !== 'undefined') return false\\n      }\\n    })\\n    return value\\n  }\\n\\n  rawValue(node, prop) {\\n    let value = node\[prop]\\n    let raw = node.raws\[prop]\\n    if (raw \&\& raw.value === value) {\\n      return raw.raw\\n    }\\n\\n    return value\\n  }\\n\\n  root(node) {\\n    this.body(node)\\n    if (node.raws.after) {\\n      let after = node.raws.after\\n      let isDocument = node.parent \&\& node.parent.type === 'document'\\n      this.builder(isDocument ? after : escapeHTMLInCSS(after))\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n &#x20;   **{"offset": {"line": 835, "column": 0}, "map": {"version":3,"sources":\["turbopack:///\[project]/node\_modules/postcss/lib/node.js"],"sourcesContent":\["'use strict'\\n\\nlet CssSyntaxError = require('./css-syntax-error')\\nlet Stringifier = require('./stringifier')\\nlet stringify = require('./stringify')\\nlet { isClean, my } = require('./symbols')\\n\\nfunction cloneNode(obj, parent) {\\n  let cloned = new obj.constructor()\\n\\n  for (let i in obj) {\\n    if (!Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(obj, i)) {\\n      /\* c8 ignore next 2 \*/\\n      continue\\n    }\\n    if (i === 'proxyCache') continue\\n    let value = obj\[i]\\n    let type = typeof value\\n\\n    if (i === 'parent' \&\& type === 'object') {\\n      if (parent) cloned\[i] = parent\\n    } else if (i === 'source') {\\n      cloned\[i] = value\\n    } else if (Array.isArray(value)) {\\n      cloned\[i] = value.map(j => cloneNode(j, cloned))\\n    } else {\\n      if (type === 'object' \&\& value !== null) value = cloneNode(value)\\n      cloned\[i] = value\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  return cloned\\n}\\n\\nfunction sourceOffset(inputCSS, position) {\\n  // Not all custom syntaxes support `offset` in `source.start` and `source.end`\\n  if (position \&\& typeof position.offset !== 'undefined') {\\n    return position.offset\\n  }\\n\\n  let column = 1\\n  let line = 1\\n  let offset = 0\\n\\n  for (let i = 0; i < inputCSS.length; i++) {\\n    if (line === position.line \&\& column === position.column) {\\n      offset = i\\n      break\\n    }\\n\\n    if (inputCSS\[i] === '\\\\n') {\\n      column = 1\\n      line += 1\\n    } else {\\n      column += 1\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  return offset\\n}\\n\\nclass Node {\\n  get proxyOf() {\\n    return this\\n  }\\n\\n  constructor(defaults = {}) {\\n    this.raws = {}\\n    this\[isClean] = false\\n    this\[my] = true\\n\\n    for (let name in defaults) {\\n      if (name === 'nodes') {\\n        this.nodes = \[]\\n        for (let node of defaults\[name]) {\\n          if (typeof node.clone === 'function') {\\n            this.append(node.clone())\\n          } else {\\n            this.append(node)\\n          }\\n        }\\n      } else {\\n        this\[name] = defaults\[name]\\n      }\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  addToError(error) {\\n    error.postcssNode = this\\n    if (error.stack \&\& this.source \&\& /\\\\n\\\\s{4}at /.test(error.stack)) {\\n      let s = this.source\\n      error.stack = error.stack.replace(\\n        /\\\\n\\\\s{4}at /,\\n        `$\&${s.input.from}:${s.start.line}:${s.start.column}$\&`\\n      )\\n    }\\n    return error\\n  }\\n\\n  after(add) {\\n    this.parent.insertAfter(this, add)\\n    return this\\n  }\\n\\n  assign(overrides = {}) {\\n    for (let name in overrides) {\\n      this\[name] = overrides\[name]\\n    }\\n    return this\\n  }\\n\\n  before(add) {\\n    this.parent.insertBefore(this, add)\\n    return this\\n  }\\n\\n  cleanRaws(keepBetween) {\\n    delete this.raws.before\\n    delete this.raws.after\\n    if (!keepBetween) delete this.raws.between\\n  }\\n\\n  clone(overrides = {}) {\\n    let cloned = cloneNode(this)\\n    for (let name in overrides) {\\n      cloned\[name] = overrides\[name]\\n    }\\n    return cloned\\n  }\\n\\n  cloneAfter(overrides = {}) {\\n    let cloned = this.clone(overrides)\\n    this.parent.insertAfter(this, cloned)\\n    return cloned\\n  }\\n\\n  cloneBefore(overrides = {}) {\\n    let cloned = this.clone(overrides)\\n    this.parent.insertBefore(this, cloned)\\n    return cloned\\n  }\\n\\n  error(message, opts = {}) {\\n    if (this.source) {\\n      let { end, start } = this.rangeBy(opts)\\n      return this.source.input.error(\\n        message,\\n        { column: start.column, line: start.line },\\n
