**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Returns an absolute path to the given module path.**

&#x20;**\* Module path should be relative, either path to a file or a directory.**

&#x20;**\***

&#x20;**\* This fn allows to calculate an absolute path for some global static values, such as**

&#x20;**\* `\_\_dirname` or `import.meta.url` that Turbopack will not embeds in compile time.**

&#x20;**\* See ImportMetaBinding::code\_generation for the usage.**

&#x20;**\*/ function resolveAbsolutePath(modulePath) {**

&#x20;   **if (modulePath) {**

&#x20;       **return path.join(ABSOLUTE\_ROOT, modulePath);**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **return ABSOLUTE\_ROOT;**

**}**

**Context.prototype.P = resolveAbsolutePath;**

**/\* eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars \*/ /// <reference path="../shared/runtime/runtime-utils.ts" />**

**function readWebAssemblyAsResponse(path) {**

&#x20;   **const { createReadStream } = require('fs');**

&#x20;   **const { Readable } = require('stream');**

&#x20;   **const stream = createReadStream(path);**

&#x20;   **// @ts-ignore unfortunately there's a slight type mismatch with the stream.**

&#x20;   **return new Response(Readable.toWeb(stream), {**

&#x20;       **headers: {**

&#x20;           **'content-type': 'application/wasm'**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;   **});**

**}**

**async function compileWebAssemblyFromPath(path) {**

&#x20;   **const response = readWebAssemblyAsResponse(path);**

&#x20;   **return await WebAssembly.compileStreaming(response);**

**}**

**async function instantiateWebAssemblyFromPath(path, importsObj) {**

&#x20;   **const response = readWebAssemblyAsResponse(path);**

&#x20;   **const { instance } = await WebAssembly.instantiateStreaming(response, importsObj);**

&#x20;   **return instance.exports;**

**}**

**/\* eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars \*/ /// <reference path="../../shared/runtime/runtime-utils.ts" />**

**/// <reference path="../../shared-node/base-externals-utils.ts" />**

**/// <reference path="../../shared-node/node-externals-utils.ts" />**

**/// <reference path="../../shared-node/node-wasm-utils.ts" />**

**/// <reference path="./nodejs-globals.d.ts" />**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Base Node.js runtime shared between production and development.**

&#x20;**\* Contains chunk loading, module caching, and other non-HMR functionality.**

&#x20;**\*/ process.env.TURBOPACK = '1';**

**const url = require('url');**

**const moduleFactories = new Map();**

**const moduleCache = Object.create(null);**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Returns an absolute path to the given module's id.**

&#x20;**\*/ function resolvePathFromModule(moduleId) {**

&#x20;   **const exported = this.r(moduleId);**

&#x20;   **const exportedPath = exported?.default ?? exported;**

&#x20;   **if (typeof exportedPath !== 'string') {**

&#x20;       **return exported;**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **const strippedAssetPrefix = exportedPath.slice(ASSET\_PREFIX.length);**

&#x20;   **const resolved = path.resolve(RUNTIME\_ROOT, strippedAssetPrefix);**

&#x20;   **return url.pathToFileURL(resolved).href;**

**}**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Exports a URL value. No suffix is added in Node.js runtime.**

&#x20;**\*/ function exportUrl(urlValue, id) {**

&#x20;   **exportValue.call(this, urlValue, id);**

**}**

**function loadRuntimeChunk(sourcePath, chunkData) {**

&#x20;   **if (typeof chunkData === 'string') {**

&#x20;       **loadRuntimeChunkPath(sourcePath, chunkData);**

&#x20;   **} else {**

&#x20;       **loadRuntimeChunkPath(sourcePath, chunkData.path);**

&#x20;   **}**

**}**

**const loadedChunks = new Set();**

**const unsupportedLoadChunk = Promise.resolve(undefined);**

**const loadedChunk = Promise.resolve(undefined);**

**const chunkCache = new Map();**

**function clearChunkCache() {**

&#x20;   **chunkCache.clear();**

&#x20;   **loadedChunks.clear();**

**}**

**function loadRuntimeChunkPath(sourcePath, chunkPath) {**

&#x20;   **if (!isJs(chunkPath)) {**

&#x20;       **// We only support loading JS chunks in Node.js.**

&#x20;       **// This branch can be hit when trying to load a CSS chunk.**

&#x20;       **return;**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **if (loadedChunks.has(chunkPath)) {**

&#x20;       **return;**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **try {**

&#x20;       **const resolved = path.resolve(RUNTIME\_ROOT, chunkPath);**

&#x20;       **const chunkModules = require(resolved);**

&#x20;       **installCompressedModuleFactories(chunkModules, 0, moduleFactories);**

&#x20;       **loadedChunks.add(chunkPath);**

&#x20;   **} catch (cause) {**

&#x20;       **let errorMessage = `Failed to load chunk ${chunkPath}`;**

&#x20;       **if (sourcePath) {**

&#x20;           **errorMessage += ` from runtime for chunk ${sourcePath}`;**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;       **const error = new Error(errorMessage, {**

&#x20;           **cause**

&#x20;       **});**

&#x20;       **error.name = 'ChunkLoadError';**

&#x20;       **throw error;**

&#x20;   **}**

**}**

**function loadChunkAsync(chunkData) {**

&#x20;   **const chunkPath = typeof chunkData === 'string' ? chunkData : chunkData.path;**

&#x20;   **if (!isJs(chunkPath)) {**

&#x20;       **// We only support loading JS chunks in Node.js.**

&#x20;       **// This branch can be hit when trying to load a CSS chunk.**

&#x20;       **return unsupportedLoadChunk;**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **let entry = chunkCache.get(chunkPath);**

&#x20;   **if (entry === undefined) {**

&#x20;       **try {**

&#x20;           **// resolve to an absolute path to simplify `require` handling**

&#x20;           **const resolved = path.resolve(RUNTIME\_ROOT, chunkPath);**

&#x20;           **// TODO: consider switching to `import()` to enable concurrent chunk loading and async file io**

&#x20;           **// However this is incompatible with hot reloading (since `import` doesn't use the require cache)**

&#x20;           **const chunkModules = require(resolved);**

&#x20;           **installCompressedModuleFactories(chunkModules, 0, moduleFactories);**

&#x20;           **entry = loadedChunk;**

&#x20;       **} catch (cause) {**

&#x20;           **const errorMessage = `Failed to load chunk ${chunkPath} from module ${this.m.id}`;**

&#x20;           **const error = new Error(errorMessage, {**

&#x20;               **cause**

&#x20;           **});**

&#x20;           **error.name = 'ChunkLoadError';**

&#x20;           **// Cache the failure promise, future requests will also get this same rejection**

&#x20;           **entry = Promise.reject(error);**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;       **chunkCache.set(chunkPath, entry);**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **// TODO: Return an instrumented Promise that React can use instead of relying on referential equality.**

&#x20;   **return entry;**

**}**

**contextPrototype.l = loadChunkAsync;**

**function loadChunkAsyncByUrl(chunkUrl) {**

&#x20;   **const path1 = url.fileURLToPath(new URL(chunkUrl, RUNTIME\_ROOT));**

&#x20;   **return loadChunkAsync.call(this, path1);**

**}**

**contextPrototype.L = loadChunkAsyncByUrl;**

**function loadWebAssembly(chunkPath, \_edgeModule, imports) {**

&#x20;   **const resolved = path.resolve(RUNTIME\_ROOT, chunkPath);**

&#x20;   **return instantiateWebAssemblyFromPath(resolved, imports);**

**}**

**contextPrototype.w = loadWebAssembly;**

**function loadWebAssemblyModule(chunkPath, \_edgeModule) {**

&#x20;   **const resolved = path.resolve(RUNTIME\_ROOT, chunkPath);**

&#x20;   **return compileWebAssemblyFromPath(resolved);**

**}**

**contextPrototype.u = loadWebAssemblyModule;**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Creates a Node.js worker thread by instantiating the given WorkerConstructor**

&#x20;**\* with the appropriate path and options, including forwarded globals.**

&#x20;**\***

&#x20;**\* @param WorkerConstructor The Worker constructor from worker\_threads**

&#x20;**\* @param workerPath Path to the worker entry chunk**

&#x20;**\* @param workerOptions options to pass to the Worker constructor (optional)**

&#x20;**\*/ function createWorker(WorkerConstructor, workerPath, workerOptions) {**

&#x20;   **// Build the forwarded globals object**

&#x20;   **const forwardedGlobals = {};**

&#x20;   **for (const name of WORKER\_FORWARDED\_GLOBALS){**

&#x20;       **forwardedGlobals\[name] = globalThis\[name];**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **// Merge workerData with forwarded globals**

&#x20;   **const existingWorkerData = workerOptions?.workerData || {};**

&#x20;   **const options = {**

&#x20;       **...workerOptions,**

&#x20;       **workerData: {**

&#x20;           **...typeof existingWorkerData === 'object' ? existingWorkerData : {},**

&#x20;           **\_\_turbopack\_globals\_\_: forwardedGlobals**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;   **};**

&#x20;   **return new WorkerConstructor(workerPath, options);**

**}**

**const regexJsUrl = /\\.js(?:\\?\[^#]\*)?(?:#.\*)?$/;**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Checks if a given path/URL ends with .js, optionally followed by ?query or #fragment.**

&#x20;**\*/ function isJs(chunkUrlOrPath) {**

&#x20;   **return regexJsUrl.test(chunkUrlOrPath);**

**}**

**/// <reference path="./runtime-utils.ts" />**

**/// <reference path="./runtime-types.d.ts" />**

**/// <reference path="./dev-extensions.ts" />**

**/// <reference path="./dev-protocol.d.ts" />**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Shared HMR (Hot Module Replacement) implementation.**

&#x20;**\***

&#x20;**\* This file contains the complete HMR implementation that's shared between**

&#x20;**\* browser and Node.js runtimes. It manages module hot state, dependency**

&#x20;**\* tracking, the module.hot API, and the full HMR update flow.**

&#x20;**\*/ /\*\***

&#x20;**\* The development module cache shared across the runtime.**

&#x20;**\* Browser runtime declares this directly.**

&#x20;**\* Node.js runtime assigns globalThis.\_\_turbopack\_module\_cache\_\_ to this.**

&#x20;**\*/ let devModuleCache;**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Module IDs that are instantiated as part of the runtime of a chunk.**

&#x20;**\*/ let runtimeModules;**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Maps module IDs to persisted data between executions of their hot module**

&#x20;**\* implementation (`hot.data`).**

&#x20;**\*/ const moduleHotData = new Map();**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Maps module instances to their hot module state.**

&#x20;**\* Uses WeakMap so it works with both HotModule and ModuleWithDirection.**

&#x20;**\*/ const moduleHotState = new WeakMap();**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Modules that call `module.hot.invalidate()` (while being updated).**

&#x20;**\*/ const queuedInvalidatedModules = new Set();**

**class UpdateApplyError extends Error {**

&#x20;   **name = 'UpdateApplyError';**

&#x20;   **dependencyChain;**

&#x20;   **constructor(message, dependencyChain){**

&#x20;       **super(message);**

&#x20;       **this.dependencyChain = dependencyChain;**

&#x20;   **}**

**}**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Records parent-child relationship when a module imports another.**

&#x20;**\* Should be called during module instantiation.**

&#x20;**\*/ // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars**

**function trackModuleImport(parentModule, childModuleId, childModule) {**

&#x20;   **// Record that parent imports child**

&#x20;   **if (parentModule.children.indexOf(childModuleId) === -1) {**

&#x20;       **parentModule.children.push(childModuleId);**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **// Record that child is imported by parent**

&#x20;   **if (childModule \&\& childModule.parents.indexOf(parentModule.id) === -1) {**

&#x20;       **childModule.parents.push(parentModule.id);**

&#x20;   **}**

**}**

**function formatDependencyChain(dependencyChain) {**

&#x20;   **return `Dependency chain: ${dependencyChain.join(' -> ')}`;**

**}**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Walks the dependency tree to find all modules affected by a change.**

&#x20;**\* Returns information about whether the update can be accepted and which**

&#x20;**\* modules need to be invalidated.**

&#x20;**\***

&#x20;**\* @param moduleId - The module that changed**

&#x20;**\* @param autoAcceptRootModules - If true, root modules auto-accept updates without explicit module.hot.accept().**

&#x20;**\*                           This is used for server-side HMR where pages auto-accept at the top level.**

&#x20;**\*/ function getAffectedModuleEffects(moduleId, autoAcceptRootModules) {**

&#x20;   **const outdatedModules = new Set();**

&#x20;   **const outdatedDependencies = new Map();**

&#x20;   **const queue = \[**

&#x20;       **{**

&#x20;           **moduleId,**

&#x20;           **dependencyChain: \[]**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;   **];**

&#x20;   **let nextItem;**

&#x20;   **while(nextItem = queue.shift()){**

&#x20;       **const { moduleId, dependencyChain } = nextItem;**

&#x20;       **if (moduleId != null) {**

&#x20;           **if (outdatedModules.has(moduleId)) {**

&#x20;               **continue;**

&#x20;           **}**

&#x20;           **outdatedModules.add(moduleId);**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;       **// We've arrived at the runtime of the chunk, which means that nothing**

&#x20;       **// else above can accept this update.**

&#x20;       **if (moduleId === undefined) {**

&#x20;           **if (autoAcceptRootModules) {**

&#x20;               **return {**

&#x20;                   **type: 'accepted',**

&#x20;                   **moduleId,**

&#x20;                   **outdatedModules,**

&#x20;                   **outdatedDependencies**

&#x20;               **};**

&#x20;           **}**

&#x20;           **return {**

&#x20;               **type: 'unaccepted',**

&#x20;               **dependencyChain**

&#x20;           **};**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;       **const module = devModuleCache\[moduleId];**

&#x20;       **const hotState = moduleHotState.get(module);**

&#x20;       **if (// The module is not in the cache. Since this is a "modified" update,**

&#x20;       **// it means that the module was never instantiated before.**

&#x20;       **!module || hotState.selfAccepted \&\& !hotState.selfInvalidated) {**

&#x20;           **continue;**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;       **if (hotState.selfDeclined) {**

&#x20;           **return {**

&#x20;               **type: 'self-declined',**

&#x20;               **dependencyChain,**

&#x20;               **moduleId**

&#x20;           **};**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;       **if (runtimeModules.has(moduleId)) {**

&#x20;           **if (autoAcceptRootModules) {**

&#x20;               **continue;**

&#x20;           **}**

&#x20;           **queue.push({**

&#x20;               **moduleId: undefined,**

&#x20;               **dependencyChain: \[**

&#x20;                   **...dependencyChain,**

&#x20;                   **moduleId**

&#x20;               **]**

&#x20;           **});**

&#x20;           **continue;**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;       **for (const parentId of module.parents){**

&#x20;           **const parent = devModuleCache\[parentId];**

&#x20;           **if (!parent) {**

&#x20;               **continue;**

&#x20;           **}**

&#x20;           **const parentHotState = moduleHotState.get(parent);**

&#x20;           **// Check if parent declined this dependency**

&#x20;           **if (parentHotState?.declinedDependencies\[moduleId]) {**

&#x20;               **return {**

&#x20;                   **type: 'declined',**

&#x20;                   **dependencyChain: \[**

&#x20;                       **...dependencyChain,**

&#x20;                       **moduleId**

&#x20;                   **],**

&#x20;                   **moduleId,**

&#x20;                   **parentId**

&#x20;               **};**

&#x20;           **}**

&#x20;           **// Skip if parent is already outdated**

&#x20;           **if (outdatedModules.has(parentId)) {**

&#x20;               **continue;**

&#x20;           **}**

&#x20;           **// Check if parent accepts this dependency**

&#x20;           **if (parentHotState?.acceptedDependencies\[moduleId]) {**

&#x20;               **if (!outdatedDependencies.has(parentId)) {**

&#x20;                   **outdatedDependencies.set(parentId, new Set());**

&#x20;               **}**

&#x20;               **outdatedDependencies.get(parentId).add(moduleId);**

&#x20;               **continue;**

&#x20;           **}**

&#x20;           **// Neither accepted nor declined — propagate to parent**

&#x20;           **queue.push({**

&#x20;               **moduleId: parentId,**

&#x20;               **dependencyChain: \[**

&#x20;                   **...dependencyChain,**

&#x20;                   **moduleId**

&#x20;               **]**

&#x20;           **});**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;       **// If no parents and we're at a root module, auto-accept if configured**

&#x20;       **if (module.parents.length === 0 \&\& autoAcceptRootModules) {**

&#x20;           **continue;**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **return {**

&#x20;       **type: 'accepted',**

&#x20;       **moduleId,**

&#x20;       **outdatedModules,**

&#x20;       **outdatedDependencies**

&#x20;   **};**

**}**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Merges source dependency map into target dependency map.**

&#x20;**\*/ function mergeDependencies(target, source) {**

&#x20;   **for (const \[parentId, deps] of source){**

&#x20;       **const existing = target.get(parentId);**

&#x20;       **if (existing) {**

&#x20;           **for (const dep of deps){**

&#x20;               **existing.add(dep);**

&#x20;           **}**

&#x20;       **} else {**

&#x20;           **target.set(parentId, new Set(deps));**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;   **}**

**}**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Computes all modules that need to be invalidated based on which modules changed.**

&#x20;**\***

&#x20;**\* @param invalidated - The modules that have been invalidated**

&#x20;**\* @param autoAcceptRootModules - If true, root modules auto-accept updates without explicit module.hot.accept()**

&#x20;**\*/ function computedInvalidatedModules(invalidated, autoAcceptRootModules) {**

&#x20;   **const outdatedModules = new Set();**

&#x20;   **const outdatedDependencies = new Map();**

&#x20;   **for (const moduleId of invalidated){**

&#x20;       **const effect = getAffectedModuleEffects(moduleId, autoAcceptRootModules);**

&#x20;       **switch(effect.type){**

&#x20;           **case 'unaccepted':**

&#x20;               **throw new UpdateApplyError(`cannot apply update: unaccepted module. ${formatDependencyChain(effect.dependencyChain)}.`, effect.dependencyChain);**

&#x20;           **case 'self-declined':**

&#x20;               **throw new UpdateApplyError(`cannot apply update: self-declined module. ${formatDependencyChain(effect.dependencyChain)}.`, effect.dependencyChain);**

&#x20;           **case 'declined':**

&#x20;               **throw new UpdateApplyError(`cannot apply update: declined dependency. ${formatDependencyChain(effect.dependencyChain)}. Declined by ${effect.parentId}.`, effect.dependencyChain);**

&#x20;           **case 'accepted':**

&#x20;               **for (const outdatedModuleId of effect.outdatedModules){**

&#x20;                   **outdatedModules.add(outdatedModuleId);**

&#x20;               **}**

&#x20;               **mergeDependencies(outdatedDependencies, effect.outdatedDependencies);**

&#x20;               **break;**

&#x20;           **default:**

&#x20;               **invariant(effect, (effect)=>`Unknown effect type: ${effect?.type}`);**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **return {**

&#x20;       **outdatedModules,**

&#x20;       **outdatedDependencies**

&#x20;   **};**

**}**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Creates the module.hot API object and its internal state.**

&#x20;**\* This provides the HMR API that user code calls (module.hot.accept(), etc.)**

&#x20;**\*/ function createModuleHot(moduleId, hotData) {**

&#x20;   **const hotState = {**

&#x20;       **selfAccepted: false,**

&#x20;       **selfDeclined: false,**

&#x20;       **selfInvalidated: false,**

&#x20;       **disposeHandlers: \[],**

&#x20;       **acceptedDependencies: {},**

&#x20;       **acceptedErrorHandlers: {},**

&#x20;       **declinedDependencies: {}**

&#x20;   **};**

&#x20;   **const hot = {**

&#x20;       **// TODO(alexkirsz) This is not defined in the HMR API. It was used to**

&#x20;       **// decide whether to warn whenever an HMR-disposed module required other**

&#x20;       **// modules. We might want to remove it.**

&#x20;       **active: true,**

&#x20;       **data: hotData ?? {},**

&#x20;       **accept: (modules, callback, errorHandler)=>{**

&#x20;           **if (modules === undefined) {**

&#x20;               **hotState.selfAccepted = true;**

&#x20;           **} else if (typeof modules === 'function') {**

&#x20;               **hotState.selfAccepted = modules;**

&#x20;           **} else if (typeof modules === 'object' \&\& modules !== null) {**

&#x20;               **for(let i = 0; i < modules.length; i++){**

&#x20;                   **hotState.acceptedDependencies\[modules\[i]] = callback || function() {};**

&#x20;                   **hotState.acceptedErrorHandlers\[modules\[i]] = errorHandler;**

&#x20;               **}**

&#x20;           **} else {**

&#x20;               **hotState.acceptedDependencies\[modules] = callback || function() {};**

&#x20;               **hotState.acceptedErrorHandlers\[modules] = errorHandler;**

&#x20;           **}**

&#x20;       **},**

&#x20;       **decline: (dep)=>{**

&#x20;           **if (dep === undefined) {**

&#x20;               **hotState.selfDeclined = true;**

&#x20;           **} else if (typeof dep === 'object' \&\& dep !== null) {**

&#x20;               **for(let i = 0; i < dep.length; i++){**

&#x20;                   **hotState.declinedDependencies\[dep\[i]] = true;**

&#x20;               **}**

&#x20;           **} else {**

&#x20;               **hotState.declinedDependencies\[dep] = true;**

&#x20;           **}**

&#x20;       **},**

&#x20;       **dispose: (callback)=>{**

&#x20;           **hotState.disposeHandlers.push(callback);**

&#x20;       **},**

&#x20;       **addDisposeHandler: (callback)=>{**

&#x20;           **hotState.disposeHandlers.push(callback);**

&#x20;       **},**

&#x20;       **removeDisposeHandler: (callback)=>{**

&#x20;           **const idx = hotState.disposeHandlers.indexOf(callback);**

&#x20;           **if (idx >= 0) {**

&#x20;               **hotState.disposeHandlers.splice(idx, 1);**

&#x20;           **}**

&#x20;       **},**

&#x20;       **invalidate: ()=>{**

&#x20;           **hotState.selfInvalidated = true;**

&#x20;           **queuedInvalidatedModules.add(moduleId);**

&#x20;       **},**

&#x20;       **// NOTE(alexkirsz) This is part of the management API, which we don't**

&#x20;       **// implement, but the Next.js React Refresh runtime uses this to decide**

&#x20;       **// whether to schedule an update.**

&#x20;       **status: ()=>'idle',**

&#x20;       **// NOTE(alexkirsz) Since we always return "idle" for now, these are no-ops.**

&#x20;       **addStatusHandler: (\_handler)=>{},**

&#x20;       **removeStatusHandler: (\_handler)=>{},**

&#x20;       **// NOTE(jridgewell) Check returns the list of updated modules, but we don't**

&#x20;       **// want the webpack code paths to ever update (the turbopack paths handle**

&#x20;       **// this already).**

&#x20;       **check: ()=>Promise.resolve(null)**

&#x20;   **};**

&#x20;   **return {**

&#x20;       **hot,**

&#x20;       **hotState**

&#x20;   **};**

**}**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Processes queued invalidated modules and adds them to the outdated modules set.**

&#x20;**\* Modules that call module.hot.invalidate() are queued and processed here.**

&#x20;**\***

&#x20;**\* @param outdatedModules - The current set of outdated modules**

&#x20;**\* @param autoAcceptRootModules - If true, root modules auto-accept updates without explicit module.hot.accept()**

&#x20;**\*/ function applyInvalidatedModules(outdatedModules, outdatedDependencies, autoAcceptRootModules) {**

&#x20;   **if (queuedInvalidatedModules.size > 0) {**

&#x20;       **const result = computedInvalidatedModules(queuedInvalidatedModules, autoAcceptRootModules);**

&#x20;       **for (const moduleId of result.outdatedModules){**

&#x20;           **outdatedModules.add(moduleId);**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;       **mergeDependencies(outdatedDependencies, result.outdatedDependencies);**

&#x20;       **queuedInvalidatedModules.clear();**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **return {**

&#x20;       **outdatedModules,**

&#x20;       **outdatedDependencies**

&#x20;   **};**

**}**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Computes which outdated modules have self-accepted and can be hot reloaded.**

&#x20;**\*/ function computeOutdatedSelfAcceptedModules(outdatedModules) {**

&#x20;   **const outdatedSelfAcceptedModules = \[];**

&#x20;   **for (const moduleId of outdatedModules){**

&#x20;       **const module = devModuleCache\[moduleId];**

&#x20;       **const hotState = moduleHotState.get(module);**

&#x20;       **if (module \&\& hotState?.selfAccepted \&\& !hotState.selfInvalidated) {**

&#x20;           **outdatedSelfAcceptedModules.push({**

&#x20;               **moduleId,**

&#x20;               **errorHandler: hotState.selfAccepted**

&#x20;           **});**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **return outdatedSelfAcceptedModules;**

**}**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Disposes of an instance of a module.**

&#x20;**\* Runs hot.dispose handlers and manages persistent hot data.**

&#x20;**\***

&#x20;**\* NOTE: mode = "replace" will not remove modules from devModuleCache.**

&#x20;**\* This must be done in a separate step afterwards.**

&#x20;**\*/ function disposeModule(moduleId, mode) {**

&#x20;   **const module = devModuleCache\[moduleId];**

&#x20;   **if (!module) {**

&#x20;       **return;**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **const hotState = moduleHotState.get(module);**

&#x20;   **if (!hotState) {**

&#x20;       **return;**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **const data = {};**

&#x20;   **// Run the `hot.dispose` handler, if any, passing in the persistent**

&#x20;   **// `hot.data` object.**

&#x20;   **for (const disposeHandler of hotState.disposeHandlers){**

&#x20;       **disposeHandler(data);**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **// This used to warn in `getOrInstantiateModuleFromParent` when a disposed**

&#x20;   **// module is still importing other modules.**

&#x20;   **if (module.hot) {**

&#x20;       **module.hot.active = false;**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **moduleHotState.delete(module);**

&#x20;   **// Remove the disposed module from its children's parent list.**

&#x20;   **// It will be added back once the module re-instantiates and imports its**

&#x20;   **// children again.**

&#x20;   **for (const childId of module.children){**

&#x20;       **const child = devModuleCache\[childId];**

&#x20;       **if (!child) {**

&#x20;           **continue;**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;       **const idx = child.parents.indexOf(module.id);**

&#x20;       **if (idx >= 0) {**

&#x20;           **child.parents.splice(idx, 1);**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **switch(mode){**

&#x20;       **case 'clear':**

&#x20;           **delete devModuleCache\[module.id];**

&#x20;           **moduleHotData.delete(module.id);**

&#x20;           **break;**

&#x20;       **case 'replace':**

&#x20;           **moduleHotData.set(module.id, data);**

&#x20;           **break;**

&#x20;       **default:**

&#x20;           **invariant(mode, (mode)=>`invalid mode: ${mode}`);**

&#x20;   **}**

**}**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Dispose phase: runs dispose handlers and cleans up outdated/disposed modules.**

&#x20;**\* Returns the parent modules of outdated modules for use in the apply phase.**

&#x20;**\*/ function disposePhase(outdatedModules, disposedModules, outdatedDependencies) {**

&#x20;   **for (const moduleId of outdatedModules){**

&#x20;       **disposeModule(moduleId, 'replace');**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **for (const moduleId of disposedModules){**

&#x20;       **disposeModule(moduleId, 'clear');**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **// Removing modules from the module cache is a separate step.**

&#x20;   **// We also want to keep track of previous parents of the outdated modules.**

&#x20;   **const outdatedModuleParents = new Map();**

&#x20;   **for (const moduleId of outdatedModules){**

&#x20;       **const oldModule = devModuleCache\[moduleId];**

&#x20;       **outdatedModuleParents.set(moduleId, oldModule?.parents);**

&#x20;       **delete devModuleCache\[moduleId];**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **// Remove outdated dependencies from parent module's children list.**

&#x20;   **// When a parent accepts a child's update, the child is re-instantiated**

&#x20;   **// but the parent stays alive. We remove the old child reference so it**

&#x20;   **// gets re-added when the child re-imports.**

&#x20;   **for (const \[parentId, deps] of outdatedDependencies){**

&#x20;       **const module = devModuleCache\[parentId];**

&#x20;       **if (module) {**

&#x20;           **for (const dep of deps){**

&#x20;               **const idx = module.children.indexOf(dep);**

&#x20;               **if (idx >= 0) {**

&#x20;                   **module.children.splice(idx, 1);**

&#x20;               **}**

&#x20;           **}**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **return {**

&#x20;       **outdatedModuleParents**

&#x20;   **};**

**}**

**/\* eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars \*/ /\*\***

&#x20;**\* Shared module instantiation logic.**

&#x20;**\* This handles the full module instantiation flow for both browser and Node.js.**

&#x20;**\* Only React Refresh hooks differ between platforms (passed as callback).**

&#x20;**\*/ function instantiateModuleShared(moduleId, sourceType, sourceData, moduleFactories, devModuleCache, runtimeModules, createModuleObjectFn, createContextFn, runModuleExecutionHooksFn) {**

&#x20;   **// 1. Factory validation (same in both browser and Node.js)**

&#x20;   **const id = moduleId;**

&#x20;   **const moduleFactory = moduleFactories.get(id);**

&#x20;   **if (typeof moduleFactory !== 'function') {**

&#x20;       **throw new Error(factoryNotAvailableMessage(moduleId, sourceType, sourceData) + `\\nThis is often caused by a stale browser cache, misconfigured Cache-Control headers, or a service worker serving outdated responses.` + `\\nTo fix this, make sure your Cache-Control headers allow revalidation of chunks and review your service worker configuration. ` + `As an immediate workaround, try hard-reloading the page, clearing the browser cache, or unregistering any service workers.`);**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **// 2. Hot API setup (same in both - works for browser, included for Node.js)**

&#x20;   **const hotData = moduleHotData.get(id);**

&#x20;   **const { hot, hotState } = createModuleHot(id, hotData);**

&#x20;   **// 3. Parent assignment logic (same in both)**

&#x20;   **let parents;**

&#x20;   **switch(sourceType){**

&#x20;       **case SourceType.Runtime:**

&#x20;           **runtimeModules.add(id);**

&#x20;           **parents = \[];**

&#x20;           **break;**

&#x20;       **case SourceType.Parent:**

&#x20;           **parents = \[**

&#x20;               **sourceData**

&#x20;           **];**

&#x20;           **break;**

&#x20;       **case SourceType.Update:**

&#x20;           **parents = sourceData || \[];**

&#x20;           **break;**

&#x20;       **default:**

&#x20;           **throw new Error(`Unknown source type: ${sourceType}`);**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **// 4. Module creation (platform creates base module object)**

&#x20;   **const module = createModuleObjectFn(id);**

&#x20;   **const exports = module.exports;**

&#x20;   **module.parents = parents;**

&#x20;   **module.children = \[];**

&#x20;   **module.hot = hot;**

&#x20;   **devModuleCache\[id] = module;**

&#x20;   **moduleHotState.set(module, hotState);**

&#x20;   **// 5. Module execution (React Refresh hooks are platform-specific)**

&#x20;   **try {**

&#x20;       **runModuleExecutionHooksFn(module, (refresh)=>{**

&#x20;           **const context = createContextFn(module, exports, refresh);**

&#x20;           **moduleFactory.call(exports, context, module, exports);**

&#x20;       **});**

&#x20;   **} catch (error) {**

&#x20;       **module.error = error;**

&#x20;       **throw error;**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **// 6. ESM interop (same in both)**

&#x20;   **if (module.namespaceObject \&\& module.exports !== module.namespaceObject) {**

&#x20;       **// in case of a circular dependency: cjs1 -> esm2 -> cjs1**

&#x20;       **interopEsm(module.exports, module.namespaceObject);**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **return module;**

**}**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Analyzes update entries and chunks to determine which modules were added, modified, or deleted.**

&#x20;**\* This is pure logic that doesn't depend on the runtime environment.**

&#x20;**\*/ function computeChangedModules(entries, updates, chunkModulesMap) {**

&#x20;   **const chunksAdded = new Map();**

&#x20;   **const chunksDeleted = new Map();**

&#x20;   **const added = new Map();**

&#x20;   **const modified = new Map();**

&#x20;   **const deleted = new Set();**

&#x20;   **for (const \[chunkPath, mergedChunkUpdate] of Object.entries(updates)){**

&#x20;       **switch(mergedChunkUpdate.type){**

&#x20;           **case 'added':**

&#x20;               **{**

&#x20;                   **const updateAdded = new Set(mergedChunkUpdate.modules);**

&#x20;                   **for (const moduleId of updateAdded){**

&#x20;                       **added.set(moduleId, entries\[moduleId]);**

&#x20;                   **}**

&#x20;                   **chunksAdded.set(chunkPath, updateAdded);**

&#x20;                   **break;**

&#x20;               **}**

&#x20;           **case 'deleted':**

&#x20;               **{**

&#x20;                   **const updateDeleted = chunkModulesMap ? new Set(chunkModulesMap.get(chunkPath)) : new Set();**

&#x20;                   **for (const moduleId of updateDeleted){**

&#x20;                       **deleted.add(moduleId);**

&#x20;                   **}**

&#x20;                   **chunksDeleted.set(chunkPath, updateDeleted);**

&#x20;                   **break;**

&#x20;               **}**

&#x20;           **case 'partial':**

&#x20;               **{**

&#x20;                   **const updateAdded = new Set(mergedChunkUpdate.added);**

&#x20;                   **const updateDeleted = new Set(mergedChunkUpdate.deleted);**

&#x20;                   **for (const moduleId of updateAdded){**

&#x20;                       **added.set(moduleId, entries\[moduleId]);**

&#x20;                   **}**

&#x20;                   **for (const moduleId of updateDeleted){**

&#x20;                       **deleted.add(moduleId);**

&#x20;                   **}**

&#x20;                   **chunksAdded.set(chunkPath, updateAdded);**

&#x20;                   **chunksDeleted.set(chunkPath, updateDeleted);**

&#x20;                   **break;**

&#x20;               **}**

&#x20;           **default:**

&#x20;               **throw new Error('Unknown merged chunk update type');**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **// If a module was added from one chunk and deleted from another in the same update,**

&#x20;   **// consider it to be modified, as it means the module was moved from one chunk to another**

&#x20;   **// AND has new code in a single update.**

&#x20;   **for (const moduleId of added.keys()){**

&#x20;       **if (deleted.has(moduleId)) {**

&#x20;           **added.delete(moduleId);**

&#x20;           **deleted.delete(moduleId);**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **for (const \[moduleId, entry] of Object.entries(entries)){**

&#x20;       **// Modules that haven't been added to any chunk but have new code are considered**

&#x20;       **// to be modified.**

&#x20;       **// This needs to be under the previous loop, as we need it to get rid of modules**

&#x20;       **// that were added and deleted in the same update.**

&#x20;       **if (!added.has(moduleId)) {**

&#x20;           **modified.set(moduleId, entry);**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **return {**

&#x20;       **added,**

&#x20;       **deleted,**

&#x20;       **modified,**

&#x20;       **chunksAdded,**

&#x20;       **chunksDeleted**

&#x20;   **};**

**}**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Compiles new module code and walks the dependency tree to find all outdated modules.**

&#x20;**\* Uses the evalModuleEntry function to compile code (platform-specific).**

&#x20;**\***

&#x20;**\* @param added - Map of added modules**

&#x20;**\* @param modified - Map of modified modules**

&#x20;**\* @param evalModuleEntry - Function to compile module code**

&#x20;**\* @param autoAcceptRootModules - If true, root modules auto-accept updates without explicit module.hot.accept()**

&#x20;**\*/ function computeOutdatedModules(added, modified, evalModuleEntry, autoAcceptRootModules) {**

&#x20;   **const newModuleFactories = new Map();**

&#x20;   **// Compile added modules**

&#x20;   **for (const \[moduleId, entry] of added){**

&#x20;       **if (entry != null) {**

&#x20;           **newModuleFactories.set(moduleId, evalModuleEntry(entry));**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **// Walk dependency tree to find all modules affected by modifications**

&#x20;   **const { outdatedModules, outdatedDependencies } = computedInvalidatedModules(modified.keys(), autoAcceptRootModules);**

&#x20;   **// Compile modified modules**

&#x20;   **for (const \[moduleId, entry] of modified){**

&#x20;       **newModuleFactories.set(moduleId, evalModuleEntry(entry));**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **return {**

&#x20;       **outdatedModules,**

&#x20;       **outdatedDependencies,**

&#x20;       **newModuleFactories**

&#x20;   **};**

**}**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Updates module factories and re-instantiates self-accepted modules.**

&#x20;**\* Uses the instantiateModule function (platform-specific via callback).**

&#x20;**\*/ function applyPhase(outdatedSelfAcceptedModules, newModuleFactories, outdatedModuleParents, outdatedDependencies, moduleFactories, devModuleCache, instantiateModuleFn, applyModuleFactoryNameFn, reportError) {**

&#x20;   **// Update module factories**

&#x20;   **for (const \[moduleId, factory] of newModuleFactories.entries()){**

&#x20;       **applyModuleFactoryNameFn(factory);**

&#x20;       **moduleFactories.set(moduleId, factory);**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **// TODO(alexkirsz) Run new runtime entries here.**

&#x20;   **// Call accept handlers for outdated dependencies.**

&#x20;   **// This runs BEFORE re-instantiating self-accepted modules, matching**

&#x20;   **// webpack's behavior.**

&#x20;   **for (const \[parentId, deps] of outdatedDependencies){**

&#x20;       **const module = devModuleCache\[parentId];**

&#x20;       **if (!module) continue;**

&#x20;       **const hotState = moduleHotState.get(module);**

&#x20;       **if (!hotState) continue;**

&#x20;       **// Group deps by callback, deduplicating callbacks that handle multiple deps.**

&#x20;       **// Each callback receives only the deps it was registered for.**

&#x20;       **const callbackDeps = new Map();**

&#x20;       **const callbackErrorHandlers = new Map();**

&#x20;       **for (const dep of deps){**

&#x20;           **const acceptCallback = hotState.acceptedDependencies\[dep];**

&#x20;           **if (acceptCallback) {**

&#x20;               **let depList = callbackDeps.get(acceptCallback);**

&#x20;               **if (!depList) {**

&#x20;                   **depList = \[];**

&#x20;                   **callbackDeps.set(acceptCallback, depList);**

&#x20;                   **callbackErrorHandlers.set(acceptCallback, hotState.acceptedErrorHandlers\[dep]);**

&#x20;               **}**

&#x20;               **depList.push(dep);**

&#x20;           **}**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;       **for (const \[callback, cbDeps] of callbackDeps){**

&#x20;           **try {**

&#x20;               **callback.call(null, cbDeps);**

&#x20;           **} catch (err) {**

&#x20;               **const errorHandler = callbackErrorHandlers.get(callback);**

&#x20;               **if (typeof errorHandler === 'function') {**

&#x20;                   **try {**

&#x20;                       **errorHandler(err, {**

&#x20;                           **moduleId: parentId,**

&#x20;                           **dependencyId: cbDeps\[0]**

&#x20;                       **});**

&#x20;                   **} catch (err2) {**

&#x20;                       **reportError(err2);**

&#x20;                       **reportError(err);**

&#x20;                   **}**

&#x20;               **} else {**

&#x20;                   **reportError(err);**

&#x20;               **}**

&#x20;           **}**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **// Re-instantiate all outdated self-accepted modules**

&#x20;   **for (const { moduleId, errorHandler } of outdatedSelfAcceptedModules){**

&#x20;       **try {**

&#x20;           **instantiateModuleFn(moduleId, SourceType.Update, outdatedModuleParents.get(moduleId));**

&#x20;       **} catch (err) {**

&#x20;           **if (typeof errorHandler === 'function') {**

&#x20;               **try {**

&#x20;                   **errorHandler(err, {**

&#x20;                       **moduleId,**

&#x20;                       **module: devModuleCache\[moduleId]**

&#x20;                   **});**

&#x20;               **} catch (err2) {**

&#x20;                   **reportError(err2);**

&#x20;                   **reportError(err);**

&#x20;               **}**

&#x20;           **} else {**

&#x20;               **reportError(err);**

&#x20;           **}**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;   **}**

**}**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Internal implementation that orchestrates the full HMR update flow:**

&#x20;**\* invalidation, disposal, and application of new modules.**

&#x20;**\***

&#x20;**\* @param autoAcceptRootModules - If true, root modules auto-accept updates without explicit module.hot.accept()**

&#x20;**\*/ function applyInternal(outdatedModules, outdatedDependencies, disposedModules, newModuleFactories, moduleFactories, devModuleCache, instantiateModuleFn, applyModuleFactoryNameFn, autoAcceptRootModules) {**

&#x20;   **;**

&#x20;   **({ outdatedModules, outdatedDependencies } = applyInvalidatedModules(outdatedModules, outdatedDependencies, autoAcceptRootModules));**

&#x20;   **// Find self-accepted modules to re-instantiate**

&#x20;   **const outdatedSelfAcceptedModules = computeOutdatedSelfAcceptedModules(outdatedModules);**

&#x20;   **// Run dispose handlers, save hot.data, clear caches**

&#x20;   **const { outdatedModuleParents } = disposePhase(outdatedModules, disposedModules, outdatedDependencies);**

&#x20;   **let error;**

&#x20;   **function reportError(err) {**

&#x20;       **if (!error) error = err; // Keep first error**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **applyPhase(outdatedSelfAcceptedModules, newModuleFactories, outdatedModuleParents, outdatedDependencies, moduleFactories, devModuleCache, instantiateModuleFn, applyModuleFactoryNameFn, reportError);**

&#x20;   **if (error) {**

&#x20;       **throw error;**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **// Recursively apply any queued invalidations from new module execution**

&#x20;   **if (queuedInvalidatedModules.size > 0) {**

&#x20;       **applyInternal(new Set(), new Map(), \[], new Map(), moduleFactories, devModuleCache, instantiateModuleFn, applyModuleFactoryNameFn, autoAcceptRootModules);**

&#x20;   **}**

**}**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Main entry point for applying an ECMAScript merged update.**

&#x20;**\* This is called by both browser and Node.js runtimes with platform-specific callbacks.**

&#x20;**\***

&#x20;**\* @param options.autoAcceptRootModules - If true, root modules auto-accept updates without explicit**

&#x20;**\*                                   module.hot.accept(). Used for server-side HMR where pages**

&#x20;**\*                                   auto-accept at the top level.**

&#x20;**\*/ function applyEcmascriptMergedUpdateShared(options) {**

&#x20;   **const { added, modified, disposedModules, evalModuleEntry, instantiateModule, applyModuleFactoryName, moduleFactories, devModuleCache, autoAcceptRootModules } = options;**

&#x20;   **const { outdatedModules, outdatedDependencies, newModuleFactories } = computeOutdatedModules(added, modified, evalModuleEntry, autoAcceptRootModules);**

&#x20;   **applyInternal(outdatedModules, outdatedDependencies, disposedModules, newModuleFactories, moduleFactories, devModuleCache, instantiateModule, applyModuleFactoryName, autoAcceptRootModules);**

**}**

**/\* eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars \*/ /// <reference path="./runtime-base.ts" />**

**/// <reference path="../../shared/runtime/dev-extensions.ts" />**

**/// <reference path="../../shared/runtime/hmr-runtime.ts" />**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Development Node.js runtime.**

&#x20;**\* Uses HotModule and shared HMR logic for hot module replacement support.**

&#x20;**\*/ // Cast the module cache to HotModule for development mode**

**// (hmr-runtime.ts declares devModuleCache as `let` variable expecting assignment)**

**// This is safe because HotModule extends Module**

**devModuleCache = moduleCache;**

**// this is read in runtime-utils.ts so it creates a module with direction for hmr**

**createModuleWithDirectionFlag = true;**

**if (!globalThis.\_\_turbopack\_runtime\_modules\_\_) {**

&#x20;   **globalThis.\_\_turbopack\_runtime\_modules\_\_ = new Set();**

**}**

**runtimeModules = globalThis.\_\_turbopack\_runtime\_modules\_\_;**

**const nodeDevContextPrototype = Context.prototype;**

**nodeDevContextPrototype.q = exportUrl;**

**nodeDevContextPrototype.M = moduleFactories;**

**nodeDevContextPrototype.c = devModuleCache;**

**nodeDevContextPrototype.R = resolvePathFromModule;**

**nodeDevContextPrototype.b = createWorker;**

**nodeDevContextPrototype.C = clearChunkCache;**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Instantiates a module in development mode using shared HMR logic.**

&#x20;**\*/ function instantiateModule(id, sourceType, sourceData) {**

&#x20;   **// Node.js: creates base module object (hot API added by shared code)**

&#x20;   **const createModuleObjectFn = (moduleId)=>{**

&#x20;       **return createModuleWithDirection(moduleId);**

&#x20;   **};**

&#x20;   **// Node.js: creates Context (no refresh parameter)**

&#x20;   **const createContext = (module1, exports, \_refresh)=>{**

&#x20;       **return new Context(module1, exports);**

&#x20;   **};**

&#x20;   **// Node.js: no hooks wrapper, just execute directly**

&#x20;   **const runWithHooks = (module1, exec)=>{**

&#x20;       **exec(undefined); // no refresh context**

&#x20;   **};**

&#x20;   **// Use shared instantiation logic (includes hot API setup)**

&#x20;   **const newModule = instantiateModuleShared(id, sourceType, sourceData, moduleFactories, devModuleCache, runtimeModules, createModuleObjectFn, createContext, runWithHooks);**

&#x20;   **newModule.loaded = true;**

&#x20;   **return newModule;**

**}**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Instantiates a runtime module in development mode.**

&#x20;**\*/ function instantiateRuntimeModule(chunkPath, moduleId) {**

&#x20;   **return instantiateModule(moduleId, SourceType.Runtime, chunkPath);**

**}**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Retrieves a module from the cache, or instantiate it as a runtime module if it is not cached.**

&#x20;**\*/ // @ts-ignore TypeScript doesn't separate this module space from the browser runtime**

**function getOrInstantiateRuntimeModule(chunkPath, moduleId) {**

&#x20;   **const module1 = devModuleCache\[moduleId];**

&#x20;   **if (module1) {**

&#x20;       **if (module1.error) {**

&#x20;           **throw module1.error;**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;       **return module1;**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **return instantiateRuntimeModule(chunkPath, moduleId);**

**}**

**/\*\***

&#x20;**\* Retrieves a module from the cache, or instantiate it if it is not cached.**

&#x20;**\* Also tracks parent-child relationships for HMR dependency tracking.**

&#x20;**\*/ // @ts-ignore**

**function getOrInstantiateModuleFromParent(id, sourceModule) {**

&#x20;   **// Track parent-child relationship**

&#x20;   **trackModuleImport(sourceModule, id, devModuleCache\[id]);**

&#x20;   **const module1 = devModuleCache\[id];**

&#x20;   **if (module1) {**

&#x20;       **if (module1.error) {**

&#x20;           **throw module1.error;**

&#x20;       **}**

&#x20;       **return module1;**

&#x20;   **}**

&#x20;   **const newModule = instantiateModule(id, SourceType.Parent, sourceModule.id);**

&#x20;   **// Track again after instantiation to ensure the relationship is recorded**

&#x20;   **trackModuleImport(sourceModule, id, newModule);**

&#x20;   **return newModule;**

**}**

**module.exports = (sourcePath)=>({**

&#x20;       **m: (id)=>getOrInstantiateRuntimeModule(sourcePath, id),**

&#x20;       **c: (chunkData)=>loadRuntimeChunk(sourcePath, chunkData)**

&#x20;   **});**

**/// <reference path="../../shared/runtime/dev-protocol.d.ts" />**

**/// <reference path="../../shared/runtime/hmr-runtime.ts" />**

**/\* eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars \*/ /\*\***

&#x20;**\* Appends the module code with //# sourceURL and //# sourceMappingURL so**

&#x20;   {"offset": {"line": 580, "column": 0}, "map": {"version":3,"sources":\["turbopack:///\[turbopack]/shared-node/base-externals-utils.ts"],"sourcesContent":\["/// <reference path=\\"../shared/runtime/runtime-utils.ts\\" />\\n\\n/// A 'base' utilities to support runtime can have externals.\\n/// Currently this is for node.js / edge runtime both.\\n/// If a fn requires node.js specific behavior, it should be placed in `node-external-utils` instead.\\n\\nasync function externalImport(id: DependencySpecifier) {\\n  let raw\\n  try {\\n    raw = await import(id)\\n  } catch (err) {\\n    // TODO(alexkirsz) This can happen when a client-side module tries to load\\n    // an external module we don't provide a shim for (e.g. querystring, url).\\n    // For now, we fail semi-silently, but in the future this should be a\\n    // compilation error.\\n    throw new Error(`Failed to load external module ${id}: ${err}`)\\n  }\\n\\n  if (raw \&\& raw.\_\_esModule \&\& raw.default \&\& 'default' in raw.default) {\\n    return interopEsm(raw.default, createNS(raw), true)\\n  }\\n\\n  return raw\\n}\\ncontextPrototype.y = externalImport\\n\\nfunction externalRequire(\\n  id: ModuleId,\\n  thunk: () => any,\\n  esm: boolean = false\\n): Exports | EsmNamespaceObject {\\n  let raw\\n  try {\\n    raw = thunk()\\n  } catch (err) {\\n    // TODO(alexkirsz) This can happen when a client-side module tries to load\\n    // an external module we don't provide a shim for (e.g. querystring, url).\\n    // For now, we fail semi-silently, but in the future this should be a\\n    // compilation error.\\n    throw new Error(`Failed to load external module ${id}: ${err}`)\\n  }\\n\\n  if (!esm || raw.\_\_esModule) {\\n    return raw\\n  }\\n\\n  return interopEsm(raw, createNS(raw), true)\\n}\\n\\nexternalRequire.resolve = (\\n  id: string,\\n  options?: {\\n    paths?: string\[]\\n  }\\n) => {\\n  return require.resolve(id, options)\\n}\\ncontextPrototype.x = externalRequire\\n"],"names":\["externalImport","id","raw","err","Error","\_\_esModule","default","interopEsm","createNS","contextPrototype","y","externalRequire","thunk","esm","resolve","options","require","x"],"mappings":"AAAA,2DAA2D;AAE3D,6DAA6D;AAC7D,sDAAsD;AACtD,qGAAqG;AAErG,eAAeA,eAAeC,EAAuB;IACnD,IAAIC;IACJ,IAAI;QACFA,MAAM,MAAM,MAAM,CAACD;IACrB,EAAE,OAAOE,KAAK;QACZ,0EAA0E;QAC1E,0EAA0E;QAC1E,qEAAqE;QACrE,qBAAqB;QACrB,MAAM,IAAIC,MAAM,CAAC,+BAA+B,EAAEH,GAAG,EAAE,EAAEE,KAAK;IAChE;IAEA,IAAID,OAAOA,IAAIG,UAAU,IAAIH,IAAII,OAAO,IAAI,aAAaJ,IAAII,OAAO,EAAE;QACpE,OAAOC,WAAWL,IAAII,OAAO,EAAEE,SAASN,MAAM;IAChD;IAEA,OAAOA;AACT;AACAO,iBAAiBC,CAAC,GAAGV;AAErB,SAASW,gBACPV,EAAY,EACZW,KAAgB,EAChBC,MAAe,KAAK;IAEpB,IAAIX;IACJ,IAAI;QACFA,MAAMU;IACR,EAAE,OAAOT,KAAK;QACZ,0EAA0E;QAC1E,0EAA0E;QAC1E,qEAAqE;QACrE,qBAAqB;QACrB,MAAM,IAAIC,MAAM,CAAC,+BAA+B,EAAEH,GAAG,EAAE,EAAEE,KAAK;IAChE;IAEA,IAAI,CAACU,OAAOX,IAAIG,UAAU,EAAE;QAC1B,OAAOH;IACT;IAEA,OAAOK,WAAWL,KAAKM,SAASN,MAAM;AACxC;AAEAS,gBAAgBG,OAAO,GAAG,CACxBb,IACAc;IAIA,OAAOC,QAAQF,OAAO,CAACb,IAAIc;AAC7B;AACAN,iBAAiBQ,CAAC,GAAGN","ignoreList":\[0]}},

&#x20;   {"offset": {"line": 621, "column": 0}, "map": {"version":3,"sources":\["turbopack:///\[turbopack]/shared-node/node-externals-utils.ts"],"sourcesContent":\["/\* eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars \*/\\n\\ndeclare var RUNTIME\_PUBLIC\_PATH: string\\ndeclare var RELATIVE\_ROOT\_PATH: string\\ndeclare var ASSET\_PREFIX: string\\n\\nconst path = require('path')\\n\\nconst relativePathToRuntimeRoot = path.relative(RUNTIME\_PUBLIC\_PATH, '.')\\n// Compute the relative path to the `distDir`.\\nconst relativePathToDistRoot = path.join(\\n  relativePathToRuntimeRoot,\\n  RELATIVE\_ROOT\_PATH\\n)\\nconst RUNTIME\_ROOT = path.resolve(\_\_filename, relativePathToRuntimeRoot)\\n// Compute the absolute path to the root, by stripping distDir from the absolute path to this file.\\nconst ABSOLUTE\_ROOT = path.resolve(\_\_filename, relativePathToDistRoot)\\n\\n/\*\*\\n \* Returns an absolute path to the given module path.\\n \* Module path should be relative, either path to a file or a directory.\\n \*\\n \* This fn allows to calculate an absolute path for some global static values, such as\\n \* `\_\_dirname` or `import.meta.url` that Turbopack will not embeds in compile time.\\n \* See ImportMetaBinding::code\_generation for the usage.\\n \*/\\nfunction resolveAbsolutePath(modulePath?: string): string {\\n  if (modulePath) {\\n &#x20;   {"offset": {"line": 642, "column": 0}, "map": {"version":3,"sources":\["turbopack:///\[turbopack]/shared-node/node-wasm-utils.ts"],"sourcesContent":\["/\* eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars \*/\\n\\n/// <reference path=\\"../shared/runtime/runtime-utils.ts\\" />\\n\\nfunction readWebAssemblyAsResponse(path: string) {\\n  const { createReadStream } = require('fs') as typeof import('fs')\\n  const { Readable } = require('stream') as typeof import('stream')\\n\\n  const stream = createReadStream(path)\\n\\n  // @ts-ignore unfortunately there's a slight type mismatch with the stream.\\n  return new Response(Readable.toWeb(stream), {\\n    headers: {\\n      'content-type': 'application/wasm',\\n    },\\n  })\\n}\\n\\nasync function compileWebAssemblyFromPath(\\n  path: string\\n): Promise<WebAssembly.Module> {\\n  const response = readWebAssemblyAsResponse(path)\\n\\n  return await WebAssembly.compileStreaming(response)\\n}\\n\\nasync function instantiateWebAssemblyFromPath(\\n  path: string,\\n  importsObj: WebAssembly.Imports\\n): Promise<Exports> {\\n  const response = readWebAssemblyAsResponse(path)\\n\\n  const { instance } = await WebAssembly.instantiateStreaming(\\n    response,\\n    importsObj\\n  )\\n\\n  return instance.exports\\n}\\n"],"names":\["readWebAssemblyAsResponse","path","createReadStream","require","Readable","stream","Response","toWeb","headers","compileWebAssemblyFromPath","response","WebAssembly","compileStreaming","instantiateWebAssemblyFromPath","importsObj","instance","instantiateStreaming","exports"],"mappings":"AAAA,oDAAoD,GAEpD,2DAA2D;AAE3D,SAASA,0BAA0BC,IAAY;IAC7C,MAAM,EAAEC,gBAAgB,EAAE,GAAGC,QAAQ;IACrC,MAAM,EAAEC,QAAQ,EAAE,GAAGD,QAAQ;IAE7B,MAAME,SAASH,iBAAiBD;IAEhC,2EAA2E;IAC3E,OAAO,IAAIK,SAASF,SAASG,KAAK,CAACF,SAAS;QAC1CG,SAAS;YACP,gBAAgB;QAClB;IACF;AACF;AAEA,eAAeC,2BACbR,IAAY;IAEZ,MAAMS,WAAWV,0BAA0BC;IAE3C,OAAO,MAAMU,YAAYC,gBAAgB,CAACF;AAC5C;AAEA,eAAeG,+BACbZ,IAAY,EACZa,UAA+B;IAE/B,MAAMJ,WAAWV,0BAA0BC;IAE3C,MAAM,EAAEc,QAAQ,EAAE,GAAG,MAAMJ,YAAYK,oBAAoB,CACzDN,UACAI;IAGF,OAAOC,SAASE,OAAO;AACzB","ignoreList":\[0]}},

&#x20;   {"offset": {"line": 663, "column": 0}, "map": {"version":3,"sources":\["turbopack:///\[turbopack]/nodejs/runtime/runtime-base.ts"],"sourcesContent":\["/\* eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars \*/\\n\\n/// <reference path=\\"../../shared/runtime/runtime-utils.ts\\" />\\n/// <reference path=\\"../../shared-node/base-externals-utils.ts\\" />\\n/// <reference path=\\"../../shared-node/node-externals-utils.ts\\" />\\n/// <reference path=\\"../../shared-node/node-wasm-utils.ts\\" />\\n/// <reference path=\\"./nodejs-globals.d.ts\\" />\\n\\n/\*\*\\n \* Base Node.js runtime shared between production and development.\\n \* Contains chunk loading, module caching, and other non-HMR functionality.\\n \*/\\n\\nprocess.env.TURBOPACK = '1'\\n\\nconst url = require('url') as typeof import('url')\\n\\nconst moduleFactories: ModuleFactories = new Map()\\nconst moduleCache: ModuleCache<Module> = Object.create(null)\\n\\n/\*\*\\n \* Returns an absolute path to the given module's id.\\n \*/\\nfunction resolvePathFromModule(\\n  this: TurbopackBaseContext<Module>,\\n  moduleId: string\\n): string {\\n  const exported = this.r(moduleId)\\n  const exportedPath = exported?.default ?? exported\\n  if (typeof exportedPath !== 'string') {\\n    return exported as any\\n  }\\n\\n  const strippedAssetPrefix = exportedPath.slice(ASSET\_PREFIX.length)\\n  const resolved = path.resolve(RUNTIME\_ROOT, strippedAssetPrefix)\\n\\n  return url.pathToFileURL(resolved).href\\n}\\n\\n/\*\*\\n \* Exports a URL value. No suffix is added in Node.js runtime.\\n \*/\\nfunction exportUrl(\\n  this: TurbopackBaseContext<Module>,\\n  urlValue: string,\\n  id: ModuleId | undefined\\n) {\\n  exportValue.call(this, urlValue, id)\\n}\\n\\nfunction loadRuntimeChunk(sourcePath: ChunkPath, chunkData: ChunkData): void {\\n  if (typeof chunkData === 'string') {\\n    loadRuntimeChunkPath(sourcePath, chunkData)\\n  } else {\\n    loadRuntimeChunkPath(sourcePath, chunkData.path)\\n  }\\n}\\n\\nconst loadedChunks = new Set<ChunkPath>()\\nconst unsupportedLoadChunk = Promise.resolve(undefined)\\nconst loadedChunk: Promise<void> = Promise.resolve(undefined)\\nconst chunkCache = new Map<ChunkPath, Promise<void>>()\\n\\nfunction clearChunkCache() {\\n  chunkCache.clear()\\n  loadedChunks.clear()\\n}\\n\\nfunction loadRuntimeChunkPath(\\n  sourcePath: ChunkPath,\\n  chunkPath: ChunkPath\\n): void {\\n  if (!isJs(chunkPath)) {\\n    // We only support loading JS chunks in Node.js.\\n    // This branch can be hit when trying to load a CSS chunk.\\n    return\\n  }\\n\\n  if (loadedChunks.has(chunkPath)) {\\n    return\\n  }\\n\\n  try {\\n    const resolved = path.resolve(RUNTIME\_ROOT, chunkPath)\\n    const chunkModules: CompressedModuleFactories = require(resolved)\\n    installCompressedModuleFactories(chunkModules, 0, moduleFactories)\\n    loadedChunks.add(chunkPath)\\n  } catch (cause) {\\n    let errorMessage = `Failed to load chunk ${chunkPath}`\\n\\n    if (sourcePath) {\\n      errorMessage += ` from runtime for chunk ${sourcePath}`\\n    }\\n\\n    const error = new Error(errorMessage, { cause })\\n    error.name = 'ChunkLoadError'\\n    throw error\\n  }\\n}\\n\\nfunction loadChunkAsync<TModule extends Module>(\\n  this: TurbopackBaseContext<TModule>,\\n  chunkData: ChunkData\\n): Promise<void> {\\n  const chunkPath = typeof chunkData === 'string' ? chunkData : chunkData.path\\n  if (!isJs(chunkPath)) {\\n    // We only support loading JS chunks in Node.js.\\n    // This branch can be hit when trying to load a CSS chunk.\\n    return unsupportedLoadChunk\\n  }\\n\\n  let entry = chunkCache.get(chunkPath)\\n  if (entry === undefined) {\\n    try {\\n      // resolve to an absolute path to simplify `require` handling\\n      const resolved = path.resolve(RUNTIME\_ROOT, chunkPath)\\n      // TODO: consider switching to `import()` to enable concurrent chunk loading and async file io\\n      // However this is incompatible with hot reloading (since `import` doesn't use the require cache)\\n      const chunkModules: CompressedModuleFactories = require(resolved)\\n      installCompressedModuleFactories(chunkModules, 0, moduleFactories)\\n      entry = loadedChunk\\n    } catch (cause) {\\n      const errorMessage = `Failed to load chunk ${chunkPath} from module ${this.m.id}`\\n      const error = new Error(errorMessage, { cause })\\n      error.name = 'ChunkLoadError'\\n\\n      // Cache the failure promise, future requests will also get this same rejection\\n      entry = Promise.reject(error)\\n    }\\n    chunkCache.set(chunkPath, entry)\\n  }\\n  // TODO: Return an instrumented Promise that React can use instead of relying on referential equality.\\n  return entry\\n}\\ncontextPrototype.l = loadChunkAsync\\n\\nfunction loadChunkAsyncByUrl<TModule extends Module>(\\n  this: TurbopackBaseContext<TModule>,\\n  chunkUrl: string\\n) {\\n  const path = url.fileURLToPath(new URL(chunkUrl, RUNTIME\_ROOT)) as ChunkPath\\n  return loadChunkAsync.call(this, path)\\n}\\ncontextPrototype.L = loadChunkAsyncByUrl\\n\\nfunction loadWebAssembly(\\n  chunkPath: ChunkPath,\\n  \_edgeModule: () => WebAssembly.Module,\\n  imports: WebAssembly.Imports\\n) {\\n  const resolved = path.resolve(RUNTIME\_ROOT, chunkPath)\\n\\n  return instantiateWebAssemblyFromPath(resolved, imports)\\n}\\ncontextPrototype.w = loadWebAssembly\\n\\nfunction loadWebAssemblyModule(\\n  chunkPath: ChunkPath,\\n  \_edgeModule: () => WebAssembly.Module\\n) {\\n  const resolved = path.resolve(RUNTIME\_ROOT, chunkPath)\\n\\n  return compileWebAssemblyFromPath(resolved)\\n}\\ncontextPrototype.u = loadWebAssemblyModule\\n\\n/\*\*\\n \* Creates a Node.js worker thread by instantiating the given WorkerConstructor\\n \* with the appropriate path and options, including forwarded globals.\\n \*\\n \* @param WorkerConstructor The Worker constructor from worker\_threads\\n \* @param workerPath Path to the worker entry chunk\\n \* @param workerOptions options to pass to the Worker constructor (optional)\\n \*/\\nfunction createWorker(\\n  WorkerConstructor: { new (path: string, options?: object): unknown },\\n  workerPath: string,\\n  workerOptions?: { workerData?: unknown; \[key: string]: unknown }\\n): unknown {\\n  // Build the forwarded globals object\\n  const forwardedGlobals: Record<string, unknown> = {}\\n  for (const name of WORKER\_FORWARDED\_GLOBALS) {\\n    forwardedGlobals\[name] = (globalThis as Record<string, unknown>)\[name]\\n  }\\n\\n  // Merge workerData with forwarded globals\\n  const existingWorkerData = workerOptions?.workerData || {}\\n  const options = {\\n    ...workerOptions,\\n    workerData: {\\n      ...(typeof existingWorkerData === 'object' ? existingWorkerData : {}),\\n      \_\_turbopack\_globals\_\_: forwardedGlobals,\\n    },\\n  }\\n\\n  return new WorkerConstructor(workerPath, options)\\n}\\n\\nconst regexJsUrl = /\\\\.js(?:\\\\?\[^#]\*)?(?:#.\*)?$/\\n/\*\*\\n \* Checks if a given path/URL ends with .js, optionally followed by ?query or #fragment.\\n \*/\\nfunction isJs(chunkUrlOrPath: ChunkUrl | ChunkPath): boolean {\\n  return regexJsUrl.test(chunkUrlOrPath)\\n}\\n"],"names":\["process","env","TURBOPACK","url","require","moduleFactories","Map","moduleCache","Object","create","resolvePathFromModule","moduleId","exported","r","exportedPath","default","strippedAssetPrefix","slice","ASSET\_PREFIX","length","resolved","path","resolve","RUNTIME\_ROOT","pathToFileURL","href","exportUrl","urlValue","id","exportValue","call","loadRuntimeChunk","sourcePath","chunkData","loadRuntimeChunkPath","loadedChunks","Set","unsupportedLoadChunk","Promise","undefined","loadedChunk","chunkCache","clearChunkCache","clear","chunkPath","isJs","has","chunkModules","installCompressedModuleFactories","add","cause","errorMessage","error","Error","name","loadChunkAsync","entry","get","m","reject","set","contextPrototype","l","loadChunkAsyncByUrl","chunkUrl","path1","fileURLToPath","URL","L","loadWebAssembly","\_edgeModule","imports","instantiateWebAssemblyFromPath","w","loadWebAssemblyModule","compileWebAssemblyFromPath","u","createWorker","WorkerConstructor","workerPath","workerOptions","forwardedGlobals","WORKER\_FORWARDED\_GLOBALS","globalThis","existingWorkerData","workerData","options","\_\_turbopack\_globals\_\_","regexJsUrl","chunkUrlOrPath","test"],"mappings":"AAAA,oDAAoD,GAEpD,8DAA8D;AAC9D,kEAAkE;AAClE,kEAAkE;AAClE,6DAA6D;AAC7D,8CAA8C;AAE9C;;;CAGC,GAEDA,QAAQC,GAAG,CAACC,SAAS,GAAG;AAExB,MAAMC,MAAMC,QAAQ;AAEpB,MAAMC,kBAAmC,IAAIC;AAC7C,MAAMC,cAAmCC,OAAOC,MAAM,CAAC;AAEvD;;CAEC,GACD,SAASC,sBAEPC,QAAgB;IAEhB,MAAMC,WAAW,IAAI,CAACC,CAAC,CAACF;IACxB,MAAMG,eAAeF,UAAUG,WAAWH;IAC1C,IAAI,OAAOE,iBAAiB,UAAU;QACpC,OAAOF;IACT;IAEA,MAAMI,sBAAsBF,aAAaG,KAAK,CAACC,aAAaC,MAAM;IAClE,MAAMC,WAAWC,KAAKC,OAAO,CAACC,cAAcP;IAE5C,OAAOb,IAAIqB,aAAa,CAACJ,UAAUK,IAAI;AACzC;AAEA;;CAEC,GACD,SAASC,UAEPC,QAAgB,EAChBC,EAAwB;IAExBC,YAAYC,IAAI,CAAC,IAAI,EAAEH,UAAUC;AACnC;AAEA,SAASG,iBAAiBC,UAAqB,EAAEC,SAAoB;IACnE,IAAI,OAAOA,cAAc,UAAU;QACjCC,qBAAqBF,YAAYC;IACnC,OAAO;QACLC,qBAAqBF,YAAYC,UAAUZ,IAAI;IACjD;AACF;AAEA,MAAMc,eAAe,IAAIC;AACzB,MAAMC,uBAAuBC,
